definitions.definition: The ability to do work, produce change, or create heat.  There are many types of energy.  For example, heat energy (also known as thermal energy) is created when molecules move around quickly and randomly within a substance, which releases heat.  Chemical energy is what holds atoms of a molecule or molecules of a substance together.  When these groups of atoms or molecules are separated during a chemical reaction, chemical energy is released, often as heat.  When organisms photosynthesize or digest food, chemical reactions provide chemical energy for their growth and development.
definitions.sentence: The sun is a source of heat energy because it warms the earth.  Plants convert light from the sun into chemical energy through photosynthesis.

translations.definition: T'áá hoolzhishgi dahináanii t'áá sahdii dahyikahgo yik'ehgo náás oołchíiłii łahgo át'é daazlį́į'ígíí t'áá ałtso ááł jiní

definitions.definition: A species that can thrive in many different environmental conditions or use different types of resources. Compare with specialist.
definitions.sentence: A raccoon is an example of a generalist.  It lives in a variety of habitats, from cities to forests, and it eats a lot of different types of food.

translations.definition: Dahináanii ła' dahyikahgo łahgo ánáhoo'níiłgi kéédahat'į́ éí doodago yee dahináanii ał'ąą ádaat'éego yee kééhát'į́

definitions.definition: All of the organisms born and living at about the same time. In humans, mother and daughters represent two generations
definitions.sentence: During elementary school, all the kids in your class are a part of your generation because all of you have similar ages.

translations.definition: Hináanii t'áá ałhąąh daazhchí dóó danihinánígíí, Amá dóó ach'é'é éí naakidi azhchínígíí át'é

definitions.definition: A disease that is caused by a change (mutation) in an individual's DNA sequence
definitions.sentence: A disease like xeroderma pigmentosum makes you sensitive to sunlight and is a genetic disorder because it can be inherited from your parents.

translations.definition: Bik'ehgo náás oochíiłii ałkéé' sinilyę́ę łahgo ádzaago ba'át'e' hazlį́į'go bits'ą́ą́dóó ąąh dahaz'ą́ bił oochííł yileeh.

definitions.definition: The deliberate modification of the characteristics of an organism by changing its DNA or RNA
definitions.sentence: There are types of corn that have had their DNA genetically engineered so that they can resist insect herbivores.  Organisms that have had their DNA engineered are called genetically modified organisms (GMOs).

translations.definition: Ha'át'íi da hináanii bits'íís dóó bíni' da yee hadít'éhígíí t'áá ákólnéehgo be'iiná bitł'óól łahgo ál'įįhígíí

definitions.definition: The study of genes and inheritance in living organisms
definitions.sentence: A scientist who studies corn genetics researches how traits and diseases are inherited and affect yield.

translations.definition: Dahináanii yik'ehgo náás oołchííłígíí dóó yił oołchíiłii nidaalkaahgo t'áá át'é bíhoo'aah

entries.literal: bik'ehgo = following, náás oochíiłii = forward birth, naalkaah = study/ research. Meaning, "study the following of reproduction"

definitions.definition: All of an organism's DNA
definitions.sentence: We can compare the genomes of different people to see if we can identify which alleles cause diseases.

translations.definition: Ha'át'íi da hináanii bits'íís bitł'óól t'áá ałtso bits'íís bii' hólónígíí

short_translations: hái da bi allele t'áá ałtso

definitions.definition: All of the alleles in a given individual's genome
definitions.sentence: A sheep's genotype may include alleles for white wool and black wool, but because the black allele is dominant, the sheep's phenotype will be black wool.

translations.definition: Ha'át'íi da hináanii yik'ehgo náás oołchíiłii łahgo át'é daazlį́į́'ígíí t'áá ałtso

entries.word: hái da bi allele t'áá ałtso

definitions.definition: The science that deals with the earth's physical structure, the materials it is made of, its history, and the processes that act on it.
definitions.sentence: A scientist who studies geology can research how structures like mountains and canyons were formed.

translations.definition: Nahasdzáán ninálkaahgo ha'át'íi da yee hadít'éhígíí, biyi'di dóó bikáá'góó hólónígíí dahazlį́į́'dę́ę́' dóó yee naalnishígíí bíhoo'aah

entries.literal: nahasdzáán = earth, naalkaah = study

definitions.definition: The increase in the Earth's average temperature. Recently, temperatures are rising rapidly due to increases in the levels of greenhouse gases.
definitions.sentence: One of the results of burning fossil fuels is global warming, which is causing a rise in the average temperature of the planet.

translations.definition: Nahasdzáán bikáá'góó sidooígíí de'áániił. Níłch'i hado yił hidideełígíí, greenhouse gases, łą́ dahshoogish silį́į́'ígíí biniinaa nahasdzáán ayóo sido silį́į́'.

definitions.definition: The simple sugar that is the main source of energy. Glucose is found in the blood and is the main sugar that the body makes.
definitions.sentence: Someone with diabetes needs to monitor their glucose levels, which is the amount of sugar that is in their bloodstream.

translations.definition: Łikání hadił bitahdi hólǫ́ǫgo íiyisíí bee hadziil dóó hwiinéí áhósinígíí óolyé. Łikání glucose wolyéhígíí hadił bitahdi hólǫ́ éí hats'íís ííł'į́įgo hadił bitahdi niheleeh.

entries.literal: chʼiyáán = food, bee jiinánígíí = keeping you alive... means "something about the food is keeping you alive"

definitions.definition: Any gas that can absorb heat from Earth's surface and keeps it from leaving the atmosphere into space.
definitions.sentence: Greenhouse gases act like a blanket around Earth, trapping heat instead of letting it escape from the atmosphere.

translations.definition: Níłch'i nahasdzáán bikáá'góó náhoniidohígíí yił hidideeł dóó doo yiidichxíid dago biniinaa doo nahasdzáán bits'ą́ąjį' aheldóoh da.

definitions.definition: Place where an organism or a community of organisms lives, including all living and nonliving factors or conditions of the environment
definitions.sentence: Some animal species can only survive in a specific habitat, like roadrunners that live in the deserts, brushlands, and grasslands of North America.

translations.definition: Dahináanii kééhat'į́įgi áádóó ákwe'é haz'ą́ągi t'áá ałtsoní bee hahodít'éego haz'ą́

definitions.definition: A cell or organism having a single set of chromosomes
definitions.sentence: Unlike most cells in our body, egg and sperm cells are haploid and have one copy of each chromosome.

translations.definition: Hinááts'ózí éí doodago hináanii bits'íístahdi iiná bitł'óól ałk'ídeesdizígíí chromosomes t'ááłáhájí t'éí biyi'di hólǫ́

entries.literal: iiná bitł'óól = DNA. t'áá łá'í = one. Meaning, "One DNA".

definitions.definition: Animals that eat plants
definitions.sentence: Rabbits are herbivores that only eat leaves, seeds, twigs, and other parts of plants.

translations.definition: Na'at'i'í éí doodago naaldlooshii chil deiłchozhígíí

definitions.definition: The passing on of traits from parents to offspring through genetic information.
definitions.sentence: Scientific discoveries about heredity allow scientists to identify the causes of many genetic disorders.

translations.definition: Dahináanii yizhchíigo bimá dóó bizhé'é yik'ehgo náás oołchíiłii bee bik'i' aschínígíí yee hadít'é yileeh

entries.literal: bił náás = onward/forward. oochíił = birth. Meaning, "giving birth onward"

definitions.definition: An organism that eats plants or animals for energy and nutrients (compare with autotroph)
definitions.sentence: People, insects, fish, and birds are examples of heterotrophs because they must eat plants and animals to survive.

translations.definition: Ha'át'íi da hináanii bidziil dóó yee hináadoo biniyé ch'il/nanise' dóó níláhgóó dahináanii yiyą́

short_translations: bik'ehgo náás oochíiłii naaki ał'ąą át'é

definitions.definition: An individual who has two different alleles of a gene. Compare with homozygote.
definitions.sentence: Individuals who only have one sickle cell allele are heterozygotes at that locus and will not display sickle cell disease.

translations.definition: Bíla'ashdla'ii bi'iiná bitł'óól bąąhgi bik'ehgo náás oochíiłii gene naaki ał'ąą át'éego hólónígíí

entries.word: bik'ehgo náás oochíiłii naaki ał'ąą át'é

definitions.definition: A state in which an animal’s body processes are slower than usual, its body temperature falls, and its activities are limited, usually during winter or when food is scarce.
definitions.sentence: Many animals that live in the mountains hibernate because there is little food in the cold winter months.

translations.definition: Haigo éí doodago ch'iyáán doo łą́ dahólǫ́ǫ da haleehgo na'at'i'í dóó naaldlooshii bits'íís ni'át'įįh, doo ayóo naha'ná da, doo ayóo sizil da,

definitions.definition: The ability of the body or a cell to maintain a stable internal environment despite changes in external conditions.
definitions.sentence: You sweat when it is hot and shiver when it is cold because of homeostasis. This is your body's way of staying at a constant temperature.

translations.definition: Níláhgóó ał'ąą ánáhoo'níił nidi ats'íístah di doodago hinááhts'óóz/ats'íís bitł'óól biyi'di t'ááłáhági áhoot'é.

definitions.definition: Similarity of DNA or RNA sequences, proteins, or traits due to shared ancestry
definitions.sentence: Bees, ants, and wasps share a homologous trait of three-part bodies (abdomen, thorax, and head) because they have shared ancestry.

translations.definition: Iiná bitł'óól, DNA dóó RNA t'áá áłah ałkéé' danéedééhígíí, ats'íís bee haleehii proteins, éí doodago bik'ehgo ázhnoolnin dóó ájít'é yileehígíí ahedaałt'éego

definitions.definition: An individual who has two of the same alleles for a gene. Compare with heterozygote.
definitions.sentence: Individuals who have two sickle cell alleles are homozygotes at that locus and will display sickle cell disease.

translations.definition: Bíla'ashdla'ii ła' biyik'ehgo náás oołchíiłii łahgo át'é silį́į́'ígíí naakigo bee hólǫ́.

definitions.definition: Chemical substances that act as messenger molecules in the body. After being made in one part of the body, they travel to other parts of the body where they help control how cells and organs do their work
definitions.sentence: Type II diabetes is a result of being unable to regulate blood sugar levels because the body doesn't respond to a hormone called insulin correctly.

translations.definition: Akááz bitoo' ats'íís yee ákonízinígíí. Ats'íís biyi'di haleeh aadóó ats'íístahgóó hoo'nááł yileeh.

entries.literal: akááz = gland, bitoo' = juice. Meaning,The juice of the gland

definitions.definition: Molecules and surfaces that do interact with water
definitions.sentence: Salt is hydrophilic; it is easily dissolved when it is mixed with water.

translations.definition: Ha'át'íi da bizhool,  molecules, éí doodago bikáá'góó tó bił ałtah yileehígíí

definitions.definition: Molecules and surfaces that do no interact with water
definitions.sentence: Oil is hydrophobic; it will not dissolve when mixed with water.

translations.definition: Ha'át'íi da bizhool,  molecules, éí doodago bikáá'góó tó doo bił ałtah yileehígíí

definitions.definition: A testable statement that explains a set of observations.
definitions.sentence: You think bees prefer sunny days over cloudy days because it is unlikely to rain on sunny days.  You test your hypothesis by comparing the number of bees visiting flowers on both sunny and cloudy days.

translations.definition: T'áádoole'é haalzíidgo haa da yit'ée doogo dóó bídí'nóol'ąął bohónéedzą́ baa ha'oodzíí'ígíí

definitions.definition: The movement of individuals into a particular population or area from other populations or areas. Compare with emigration.
definitions.sentence: When you get a cut it is important to wash it with soap and water so you do not get an infection.

translations.definition: Bíla'ashdla'ii tádínéehgo bíla'ashdla'ii bił haz'ą́ągo yitah yinééh éí doodago nááná łahgi haz'ą́ągi niinééhgo

definitions.definition: A collection of cells and organs that help to defend an organism against pathogens.
definitions.sentence: Someone who has the flu is infectious for the first couple of days because they can give the flu to others.

translations.definition: Hinááhts'ózí dóó akááz éí doodago atsą́ siléí bitoo' t'áádoole'é ba'át'e' dahólóonii yich'ą́ą́h naalnishígíí

definitions.definition: A single, separate organism distinguished from others of the same kind
definitions.sentence: Type II diabetes is a result of being unable to regulate blood sugar levels because the body doesn't respond to a hormone called insulin correctly.

translations.definition: Hináanii t'ááła' dahyikahígíí t'ááłá'ígo

definitions.definition: An illness caused by a pathogen, such as a virus, bacteria, or parasite.
definitions.sentence: Our immune system is made up of white blood cells and tissues like our skin which can protect us from bacterial and viral infections

translations.definition: Ba'át'e' hólóonii (pathogen) bits'ą́ą́dóó ii'noot'į́į́' doodago łóód haleehgo doo ákójít'ée da jileehígíí: naałniih (virus), ch'osh doo yit'íinii, éí doodago yaa' ádaat'éii.

definitions.definition: The process of passing on illness or disease.
definitions.sentence: One individual corn plant will produce between two and four ears of corn.

translations.definition: Ats'íís yihodiiłt'hii dóó yąąh dahooł'aahii idahidilnéehgo

definitions.definition: To receive a particular characteristic from a parent, usually through genes that are passed from parent to offspring.
definitions.sentence: When somone notices that siblings look alike, they are seeing similar traits that the siblings inherited from their parents.

translations.definition: Hamá dóó hazhé'é bits'ą́ą́dę́ę́' bik'ehgo náás oochíiłii binahjį' yee hadít'éhígíí bił ho'dizhchį́įgo

entries.literal: bił oochíiłii = birth. hazhdiidzaa = dress up. Describing " how you look as an offspring of your parents"

definitions.definition: Made of non-living materials. Compare with organic.
definitions.sentence: Inorganic substances like metal and turquoise can be used to make a turquoise bracelet.

translations.definition: Ha'át'íi da doo dahináa dago daholónígíí bee ályaa. Hináanii bits'ą́ą́dę́ę́'go éí organic át'ée doo.

entries.literal: doo = no. hináanii = living. Meaning, not living

definitions.definition: Small animals with six legs and a hard outer shell called an exoskeleton.
definitions.sentence: A ladybug is an insect because it has six legs and an outer shell; however, a tick is not an insect because it does not have six legs.

translations.definition: Hináanii ch'osh bidi'nínígíí éí hastą́ą́ bijáád dóó bits'a' dahólǫ́ǫgo yee bits'íís niłdzil éí exoskeleton wolyé.

entries.literal: ch'osh = bug

definitions.definition: An inherited behavior by animals that is not learned
definitions.sentence: Building a nest is an instinct; birds know how to build nests without being taught.

translations.definition: Ha'át'íi da hináanii áát'įįłígíí t'áá bíni' dóó bintsékees át'éego bił yizhchį́, doo t'áá bí yíhooł'ą́ą' da.

definitions.definition: The period of time when a cell grows and copies of DNA are made.
definitions.sentence: Cells spend most of their time in interphase, before making preparations to divide.

translations.definition: Hinááhts'óóz dínéesą́ą́dóó biyi'di iiná bitł'óól bééda'alne' silį́į'jį

definitions.definition: Located or occurring within a cell or cells. Compare with intercellular.
definitions.sentence: If the rooms of your house are cells, the hallways between the rooms are like intercellular space.

translations.definition: Hinááts'ózí bita'góó

definitions.definition: A species that was introduced to an ecosystem that was not there before and that causes hardship to other organisms Haa
definitions.sentence: Russian olive trees and buffelgrass are invasive species that outcompete native species and negatively affect of the environment.

translations.definition: Haa'í da t'áá íídą́ą́' yá'át'éehgo haz'ą́ągo hináanii nááná łahdę́ę́' yikahgo yah ee'nil nít'ę́ę́' ákwe'é dahinánígíí dóó haz'ánígíí yich'į' nidahwiilná silį́įgo

definitions.definition: Any animal lacking a spinal cord (also known as a backbone). Compare with vertebrate.
definitions.sentence: Worms, bees, and snails are all invertebrates.

translations.definition: Hináanii bíígháán ádaadinígíí.

definitions.definition: Atom or molecule with an electric charge
definitions.sentence: A neutral atom has equal amounts of positive and negative charges, but an atom that becomes more positively or negatively charged is an ion.

translations.definition: Atom atsiniltł'ish bąąh hólónígíí

definitions.definition: A place for science experiments, teaching, or the making of medicine or chemicals
definitions.sentence: Any space set aside for performing science experiments is a laboratory. This could even include your own backyard.

translations.definition: Haa'í da haz'ą́ągo ákone' éé'deetįįh biniyé nida'alkaah, nida'nitin, éí doodago azee' dóó t'áá ha'át'ííshį́į́ béésh da, tó da, níłch'i da ałtah ádaalne'go bee t'áadoole'é ádaalne'

definitions.definition: Any organic material that does not dissolve in water but will dissolve in organic materials.  They are often used to make cell membranes.
definitions.sentence: Butter, lard, and oil are examples of lipids, which make up our cell membranes.

translations.definition: Hináanii bits'ą́ą́dę́ę́' dahólónígíí doo tó bee nídí'eełii ákondi t'áá yił ahedaałt'éhígíí éí yee nídí'eeł. Bee hinááhts'ózí bik'ésti'ígíí nidahaleeh.

definitions.definition: Extinction of a large number of species within a short period of time
definitions.sentence: Mammals live in a variety of different environments. For example, horses run on land, whales swim in the ocean, and bats fly through the sky.

translations.definition: Hináanii ła' dahyikah nít'ę́ę́' t'áa hodina'í noonééł

definitions.definition: When a single cell divides twice to produce four cells containing half the original amount of genetic information. This is how gametes are made.
definitions.sentence: Scientists are concerned that climate change could cause a mass extinction event, similar to the one that occurred after the last Ice Age.

translations.definition: Hinááhts'ózí naakidi ałts'ánízéehgo dį́į́' yileeh. T'áá dį́į'go bik'ehgo náás oochííł bibee haz'áanii t'áá ałní'ídóó dabiyi' yileeh. Díí ak'eelyéí bee i'niitsąąhii haleeh.

short_translations: hinááhts'ózí ałts'á níséego iiná bitł'óól ałkéé' niikah

definitions.definition: The time during mitosis or meiosis where copies of chromosomes line up in the center of a cell

translations.definition: Hinááhts'ózí t'ááłáhádi éí doodago naakidi ałts'áníséego iiná bitł'óól ałníi'gi hasht'e' niikáahgo hodinaahígíí.

entries.word: hinááhts'ózí ałts'á níséego iiná bitł'óól ałkéé' niikah

definitions.definition: An organism too small to be seen without a microscope
definitions.sentence: Sperm and egg cells are created through meiosis.

translations.definition: Hináanii doo yit'íinii. Bee adéest'į́į́' dabidziilígíí t'éí bee daat'į́.

entries.literal: bug you can't see

definitions.definition: A device that makes very small objects or organisms larger so that they can be observed
definitions.sentence: You need a microscope to see cells dividing.

translations.definition: Bee adéest'į́į́' ha'át'íi da áłts'íísígo doo yit'į́į da nidi nitsaago daat'į́įgo ádeile'

entries.literal: doo yit'íinii = what you can't see. bee nél'íní = looking at with. Meaning, "looking with at what you can't see"

definitions.definition: The movement of individuals in and out of a population or area
definitions.sentence: To find food, geese migrate to the north during the summer and migrate back to the south during the winter.

translations.definition: Bíla'ashdla'ii kéédahat'į́įgi yah ahekááh dóó ch'éédahakááh.

definitions.definition: When one organism looks, sounds, or behaves like another species.
definitions.sentence: Stick insects use mimicry to trick predators into thinking they are twigs.

translations.definition: Ha'át'íi da hináanii nááná ła' hináanii yenoolnin, biinéé' yił aheełt'é, éí doodago yeel'į́įgo naaghá.

definitions.definition: An inorganic substance that occurs naturally
definitions.sentence: Precious stones like onyx and turquiose are minerals.

translations.definition: Nahasdzáán biyi'dę́ę́' t'áádoole'é doo dahináanii bits'ą́ą́dę́ę́' hólónígíí

definitions.definition: An organelle that takes in nutrients, breaks them down, and creates energy (ATP) for the cell
definitions.sentence: Power plants supply electricity to your home, just like the mitochondria supplies energy to the cell.

translations.definition: Hinááhts'ózí bitsą́ siléí ch'iyáán néidił'eeł dóó yee bidziil dóó biinéí yileehígíí

definitions.definition: When a single cell divides into two identical cells
definitions.sentence: As we grow, our cells divide through mitosis to create new cells.

translations.definition: Hinááhts'ózí t'ááłá'í si'ánígíí ałts'á níséehgo naaki yileehígíí

definitions.definition: Two or more atoms that are joined because they share electrons
definitions.sentence: Water is a molecule because it is made when two hydrogen atoms and one oxygen atom combine together.

translations.definition: Atom naaki éí doodago díkwíi da ahidiilyáago binitsiniltł'ish bináhaazléí (electrons) ahiidiinił

definitions.definition: Organisms that consist of more than one cell. Compare with unicellular.
definitions.sentence: Horses, mushrooms, and cacti are multicellular organisms because they are made of many cells.

translations.definition: Hináanii bits'íís hinááhts'ózí łą́ągo yee hadít'éhígíí. Ła'éí hinááhts'ózí t'ááłá'ígo bits'íís nilį́.

definitions.definition: A permanent alteration in the DNA sequence
definitions.sentence: Mutations are common.  Every person has a lot of mutations, but most of them are not harmful.

translations.definition: Iiná bitł'óól łahgo ádzaa.

definitions.definition: Association between organisms of two different species in which each benefits
definitions.sentence: A mutualism occurs between people and the bacteria that live in our intestines.  The bacteria gain nutrients while they help us digest food.

translations.definition: Hináanii doo t'ááła' nilį́į da nidi ahíłká aná'ahinilchxéehgo

definitions.definition: The process where organisms with the highest fitness in an environment survive and reproduce and their phenotypes become more common in the next generation. At the same time, organisms with the lowest fitness fail to survive or reproduce less and their phenotypes become less common in the next generation.
definitions.sentence: Natural selection will favor desert mice with light fur because they are less likely to be seen by predators.

translations.definition: Hoolzhishgo ał'ąą ánáhoo'níiłgo hináanii yídaníłdzilígíí náás yikah dóó oołchíiłgo bits'íís hadidzaaígíí ayóo yit'į́. Łahgo át'éego éí hináanii bił áhoot'éhígíí doo yíníłdzilgóó éí t'óó áádįįł dóó bits'íís doo łahgo áániił da doo.

definitions.definition: When the outcome of a process reduces further change and the process becomes stable
definitions.sentence: The thermostat functions as a negative feedback loop because it keeps the temperature within a building stable by turning on the air conditioning if the temperature gets too hot or turning it off if it gets too cold.

translations.definition: Ha'át'íi da naalnishgo hoolzhish nít'ę́ę́' ni'áániił silį́į́' dóó bits'ą́ą́dóó doo łahgo áhooniił da yileeh éí náasdi hasht'e didooteeł

definitions.definition: Nerves are a collection of neurons that carry signals throughout the body
definitions.sentence: Parkinson's disease is a disorder of the nervous system that occurs when neurons start to die, which affects the body's movement

translations.definition: Ats'óóz t'áá łá'í nít'i'ígíí ats'íístahdi łą́ągo ahidadiit'i'go ats'íís yee ákonízin

entries.literal: nerve

definitions.definition: The brain, spinal cord, and nerves of an animal that together receive messages from other parts of the body and control how the body functions
definitions.sentence: Each of your fingertips has over 3,000 neurons which send signals to your brain about the things you touch or feel.

translations.definition: Atsiighąą', atsiighąą' íígháán bii'nít'i'ígíí dóó ats'óóz t'áá ałtso hináanii bits'íís yee hadít'éhígíí ats'íístahgóó da'ahi'niihgo ats'íís hiná.

definitions.definition: Cells that carry electrical charges through the body and are part of the nervous system. Also known as a nerve cell.
definitions.sentence: Neutrons have the same weight as protons but have no charge. Together with protons, they form the nucleus of the atom and determine its weight.

translations.definition: Ats'óóz t'áá łá'í nít'i'ígíí bitsiniltł'ish bąąhgóó hólǫ́. Nerve cell ałdó' deiłní.

definitions.definition: An organism's position along a food chain
definitions.sentence: Plants are at the base of each food chain.  Animals that eat plants, like deer, are one trophic level above plants.  Animals that eat deer, like mountain lions, are one trophic level above deer.

translations.definition: Hináanii ayą́ągo hiná dóó áadi ha'át'ííshį́į́ bich'iiyą' nánídlį́į́góne'

definitions.definition: Organisms that consist of one cell. Compare with multicellular.
definitions.sentence: Bacteria are unicellular organisms because they are made of one cell.

translations.definition: Hináanii hinááhts'óóz t'ááłá'ígo bits'íís nilį́. Hináanii náádaała' éí hinááhts'óóz łą́ągo yee bits'íís dahólǫ́.

definitions.definition: The organs that remove waste from the body.
definitions.sentence: The urinary system is the filter of the bloodstream as it captures waste and releases it from the body.

translations.definition: Ats'íís biyi'di siláhígíí ats'íís ałtso choyoos'įįdígíí níláhgóó yits'ą́ąjį' kwííł'ínígíí

definitions.definition: An animal that has a spinal cord and a skeleton. Compare with invetebrate.
definitions.sentence: Humans, horses, and birds are vertebrates, but an octopus has no bones or spinal cord and is thus an invertebrate.

translations.definition: Hináanii naaldeehii, animals, bits'íísts'in dóó bitsiighąą' bíígháán bii' danít'i' ígíí

definitions.definition: Cells, tissues, or organs in a body that have no apparent function and is similar to a functioning organ or structure in an ancestor.
definitions.sentence: The wisdom teeth of our ancestors would replace any teeth that fell out or were removed.  Now, our wisdom teeth are vestigial traits because we have better mouth hygeine and dental care.

translations.definition: Hinááhts'óóz ats'íís biyi'di binaanish t'ááła'ígo hólónígíí, tissues, ats'ą́ siláii ats'íís biyi'di hólǫ́ nidi doo choo'į́į dago yits'ą́ą́dę́ę́' yigáałii bits'íís hólónígíí ła' choyooł'į́ dóó binaanish hólónígíí t'áá yił aheełt'é

definitions.definition: A small parasite that lives within the cells of other organisms, cannot reproduce without the help of the cell it lives inside of, and causes disease.
definitions.sentence: Hantavirus is a virus that causes serious lung disease. You can catch hantavirus from the saliva, urine, or droppings of deer mice and other rodents.

translations.definition: Hináanii doo yit'į́į dago nááná łahdę́ę́' yigáałgo ats'íís bihinááhts'óóz biyi'di hiná silį́į́', dóó áadi hinááhts'óóz yee binahjį' noosééł yileeh áádóó bits'ą́ą́dóó ąąh dahaz'ą́ haleeh

definitions.definition: The movement of water on, above, or below the surface of our planet.
definitions.sentence: The water cycle describes how water moves around the earth.  Water moves around when it rains, when rivers transport it long distances, when it evaporates from the surface of leaves, or when ocean currents move it around the globe.

translations.definition: Tó nahasdzáán bikáá'góó dóó biyi' hólǫ́  dóó hót'ááhgóó dahólǫ́ éí hoo'nááł dóó danílínígíí bił ahééhwiizhíísh

definitions.definition: Cells that are part of the body's immune system that help the body fight infection and other diseases.
definitions.sentence: White blood cells are part of the immune system. There are many different types of white blood cells circulating in your blood and each type has a specific role in fighting infections.

translations.definition: Hinááhts'óóz ats'íís yee bihodééłníi da áyósin éí ii'noot'į́į́', infection, dóó ąąh dahwiidooł'aałii yich'ą́ą́h naalnish

entries.literal: dił = blood. łigaaî = white. Meaning, white blood.

definitions.definition: The scientific study of animals
definitions.sentence: Ranchers use their zoology knowledge to breed sheep so their offspring have more meat or fluffier wool.

translations.definition: Hináanii naaldeehii, animals, ał'ąą át'éego dahyikahígíí haalzííd dóó naalkaahgo bíhoo'aah

entries.literal: naaldeehii = animal. naalkaah = study. Meaning, studying animals

definitions.definition: The cell that forms after gametes are joined.
definitions.sentence: The zygote forms once the sperm cell enters the egg cell and they become one cell.  Identical twins are a result of a zygote splitting into two cells.

translations.definition: Amá dóó azhé'é bik'eelyéí bee i'niitsąądgo

entries.literal: baby.

definitions.definition: Ha'át'íi da atom t'ááłá'ígo bee ályaaígíí. Atom t'ááłá'i yileehgo doo tanáó'níiłgo ha'át'íi da náádoodleeł da.

translations.definition: A pure substance that is made of only one type of atom. Elements cannot be broken down into any simpler substance.
translations.sentence: Elements are made up of a group of atoms that are all the same, just like a field of corn is made up of many individual corn plants.

definitions.definition: Ats'íís haashį́į́ yidzaago doo hazhó'ó naalnish da silį́į'go bee bihodiit'i'

translations.definition: A change in a living body that prevents it from functioning normally.
translations.sentence: When many think about diseases we first think about cancer or the flu. However, it is important to not forget about mental health diseases like depression or anxiety.

definitions.definition: Ats'íís yihodiiłt'hii dóó yąąh dahooł'aahii idahidilnéehgo

translations.definition: The process of passing on illness or disease.
translations.sentence: One individual corn plant will produce between two and four ears of corn.

definitions.definition: Ááhoo'nííł éí bee hahodilyaii t'áá ałtso nahasdzáán dóó yádiłhił bii' siláhígíí ahił nidaalnishgo nináháhááhígíí éí nayiił'ná hadoh dóó hak'az ádaat'éii.

translations.definition: Any non living factor that impacts an ecosystem. For example, temperature.
translations.sentence: Plant growth can be affected by abiotic factors like sunlight, water, and temperature.

definitions.definition: Ha'át'éegi da haz'ą́ągi t'áá ałtsoní hasht'e dadiit'eeh dóó ha'át'íi da doo ba'át'e' hólǫ́ǫ da yileeh. Ha'át'íi da doo ałk'ijį dooldził da, ahíhoneel'ą́ haleeh.

translations.definition: A state of balance or stable situation where opposite effects cancel each other out and no changes are occuring.
translations.sentence: A see-saw is in equilibrium when both kids are perfectly balanced on it.

ahoodziil.literal: ’ahóodziil = energy. (you modify the a depending what you're talking about)

definitions.definition: T'áádoole'é biziil yee biinéí dóó bidziilgo yee naalnish, ha'át'íi da łahgo áyooliił, doodago hadoh bits'ą́ą́dóó hólǫ́ (thermal energy). Łą́ ał'ąą át'é.

translations.definition: The ability to do work, produce change, or create heat.  There are many types of energy.  For example, heat energy (also known as thermal energy) is created when molecules move around quickly and randomly within a substance, which releases heat.  Chemical energy is what holds atoms of a molecule or molecules of a substance together.  When these groups of atoms or molecules are separated during a chemical reaction, chemical energy is released, often as heat.  When organisms photosynthesize or digest food, chemical reactions provide chemical energy for their growth and development.
translations.sentence: The sun is a source of heat energy because it warms the earth.  Plants convert light from the sun into chemical energy through photosynthesis.

definitions.definition: Ha'át'íi da hináanii áát'įįłígíí t'áá bíni' dóó bintsékees át'éego bił yizhchį́, doo t'áá bí yíhooł'ą́ą' da.

translations.definition: An inherited behavior by animals that is not learned
translations.sentence: Building a nest is an instinct; birds know how to build nests without being taught.

akaaz-bitoo.literal: akááz = gland, bitoo' = juice. Meaning,The juice of the gland

definitions.definition: Akááz bitoo' ats'íís yee ákonízinígíí. Ats'íís biyi'di haleeh aadóó ats'íístahgóó hoo'nááł yileeh.

translations.definition: Chemical substances that act as messenger molecules in the body. After being made in one part of the body, they travel to other parts of the body where they help control how cells and organs do their work
translations.sentence: Type II diabetes is a result of being unable to regulate blood sugar levels because the body doesn't respond to a hormone called insulin correctly.

akagi.literal: akágí = skin (you modify the a depending what you're talking about)

definitions.definition: Hináanii bits'íís bitł'óól (cells) bikágí
Ats'íís yee hadít'éii, organ, ats'íís bik'ésti'ígíí

translations.definition: The outermost layer of cells of any multicellular organism
translations.sentence: Your skin is your epidermis and it protects the inside of your body from harm.

translations.definition: An organ that forms the outer protective layer of the body.
translations.sentence: Our skin protects our organs from the environment by blocking moisture, preventing heat loss, and protecting us from pathogens.   Repeated sun exposure is a major cause of skin cancer.

definitions.definition: Ak'ah bineestiin dóó protein naneesdizgo akágí nilį́įgo t'áádoole'é bikí daasti'

translations.definition: A network of lipids and proteins that forms the boundary between the inside of an organelle and the rest of the space within the cell.
translations.sentence: The plasma membrane is like the "skin" of organelles like mitochondria.  It separates the inside of the organelle from the surrounding environment.

definitions.definition: Hináanii bits'ą́ą́dę́ę́' dahólónígíí doo tó bee nídí'eełii ákondi t'áá yił ahedaałt'éhígíí éí yee nídí'eeł. Bee hinááhts'ózí bik'ésti'ígíí nidahaleeh.

translations.definition: Any organic material that does not dissolve in water but will dissolve in organic materials.  They are often used to make cell membranes.
translations.sentence: Butter, lard, and oil are examples of lipids, which make up our cell membranes.

definitions.definition: Bik'ehgo náás oochíiłii łahgo át'é silį́į́'ígíí, allele, akéédóó naalnishgo bik'ehgo ats'íís haidiił'įįh béé'ályaago naaki hwee hólónígíí.

translations.definition: The allele that only determines the phenotype when the organism has two copies of it. Compare with dominant.
translations.sentence: You have two copies of each chromosome.  Sometimes there is a recessive allele on one chromosome and a dominant allele on the other.  The recessive allele is the one that will not influence the phenotype.

definitions.definition: Hinááhts'ózí naakidi ałts'ánízéehgo dį́į́' yileeh. T'áá dį́į'go bik'ehgo náás oochííł bibee haz'áanii t'áá ałní'ídóó dabiyi' yileeh. Díí ak'eelyéí bee i'niitsąąhii haleeh.
Hináanii biką'ígíí bik'eelyéí. Dabi'áádígíí ałdó' bik'eelyéí hólǫ́, biyeezhii (egg) deiłní.

translations.definition: When a single cell divides twice to produce four cells containing half the original amount of genetic information. This is how gametes are made.
translations.sentence: Scientists are concerned that climate change could cause a mass extinction event, similar to the one that occurred after the last Ice Age.

translations.definition: The male sex cell, or gamete in animals.  Compare with egg.
translations.sentence: In many species, males pass on their DNA to their offspring through their sperm cells.

definitions.definition: Hináanii biką' dóó bi'áádígíí bik'eelyéí ahidiina'go bits'ą́ą́dóó biyázhí iilts'ą'ígíí.

translations.definition: The process of combinine the male sperm with the female egg to produce a cell called a zygote.
translations.sentence: Most of the living things you see around you, including all plants and animals, are eukaryotes.

definitions.definition: Bi'áadii bik'eelyéí bihinááts'óóz biyi'di iiná bitł'óóól ałk'ídaadeesdizígíí chromosomes t'áá ałní'ígo ánéelt'e' dóó biką'ii bik'eelyéí bihinááhts'óóz biyi'di iiná bitł'óóól ałk'ídaadeesdizígíí chromosomes t'áá ałní'ígo ánéelt'e'ígíí ahidiinahgo bits'ą́ą́dóó biyázhí (zygote) bits'íís bihinááhts'óóz biyi'di iiná bitł'óól ałk'ídadeesdizígíí t'áá át'é bii' dahólǫ́.

translations.definition: A haploid reproductive cell that joins with another haploid reproductive cell of the opposite sex to form a diploid zygote.
translations.sentence: Sperm and eggs are examples of haploid cells.

akeyah.literal: would need to specify "who's land/area". Kéya is land

definitions.definition: Haa'í da haz'ą́ągo hináanii choyooł'į́įgo yaa hóchį'gi

translations.definition: Any area defended by an organism or a group of similar organisms for their own use
translations.sentence: When you boil water, the water molecules will start to move around quickly as they absorb thermal energy from the burner.

definitions.definition: Hináanii doo t'ááła' nilį́į da nidi ałch'ishdę́ę́' ahee hidi'ná

translations.definition: Any longǫterm physical relationship between individuals of two different species, including mutualism and parasitism.
translations.sentence: Scientists who study taxonomy found that whales are more closely related to animals on land (including humans) than they are to fish.

definitions.definition: Hak'éí nát'ą́ą́' ahool'áago hamá dóó hazhé'é dabishchíníinii bits'ą́ą́dę́ę́' hojizhlį́į́'ígíí

translations.definition: One from whom an individual is descended, whether on the father's or mother's side, at any number of generations.
translations.sentence: Your grandfather, great-grandmother, and great-great grandmother are all your ancestors.

definitions.definition: T'áá ha'át'ííshį́į́ bee ályaii t'ááłá'í si'ą́ągo, atom wolyé, bijéí hólǫ́, bijéí biyi'di proton biniltł'ish shábik'ehgo bii' silá éí positive deiłní dóó neutron bitsiniltł'ish bąą ádin ákondi yee óchíidii, béésh ná'iiláhá nahalingo bii' hólǫ́. Atom bijéí bii' siléí

translations.definition: The smallest component of an element having the chemical properties of the element, consisting of a nucleus containing combinations of neutrons and protons and one or more electrons
translations.sentence: All matter is made up of atoms just as a crowd is made up of people. Matter can be composed of different types of atoms, or elements, just like the people in a crowd can be men or women, old or young, and short or tall.

definitions.definition: Atoms bee ályaii shádáahjigo bitsiniltł'ish hoo'nááłígíí

translations.definition: The components of an atom that have a negative charge
translations.sentence: Electrons are the glue that hold atoms together when they combine to form a molecule.

atom-bitsiniltlish-sha-bikehgo-silaii.literal: bitsiniltł'ish = charge of the atom. shá bik'ehgo = path of the sun (meanig clockwise). siláii = laid down. Meaning "atom with a charge laid clockwise"

definitions.definition: Atoms yee hadit'éhígíí biniltł'ish shábik'ehgo, clockwise or consistent with the sun's path, siláhígíí bąąh hólǫ́

translations.definition: The components of an atom that have a positive charge
translations.sentence: The number of protons an atom has is its atomic number.  A nitrogen atom has 7 protons while an oxygen atom has 8 protons.

definitions.definition: Atom atsiniltł'ish bąąh hólónígíí

translations.definition: Atom or molecule with an electric charge
translations.sentence: A neutral atom has equal amounts of positive and negative charges, but an atom that becomes more positively or negatively charged is an ion.

atsii-bitlool-bikestii.literal: ats'íí bitł'óól  = cell. bik'ésti'í = cover. Meaning, the cover of the cell.

definitions.definition: Ats'íís bitł'óól bik'ésti'í éí ak'ah bizhool dóó ch'iyáán ats'íís hasht'ééyooldlíiłii bizhool bee ályaago ats;íís bikésti'

translations.definition: Network of lipids and proteins that forms the boundary between the inside of a cell and the environment that surrounds it.
translations.sentence: The cell membrane is like the "skin" of the cell.  It separates the inside of the cell from the surrounding environment.

atsiighaa.literal: ghąąʼ = matter. atsii = heads. So the "matter in your head"

definitions.definition: Ats'íís bii' siléí ats'íís t'áá át'é naalnishgo áyósin, ádahoot'éii t'áá ałtso yaa ákonízin dóó yénálniih, dóó bik'izhdiitáanii baa nitsídzíkees dóó háni' nilíinii yiyoołnaał

translations.definition: The organ in the body which controls the functions of the body, interprets and stores information, and affects intelligence and emotion.
translations.sentence: Your brain gives you the ability to think, speak, move, and remember.

definitions.definition: Ats'íís yee hadít'éii atsiighąą' bits'ánít'i' dóó bíígháán bii' nít'i'go ats'iighąą' dóó ats'íístahgó áhoot'éii yił ééhósin

translations.definition: Structure that begins at the the brain and continues down the spine and carries messages between the brain and the rest of the body.
translations.sentence: Starch is found in corn, potatoes, rice, and pasta.  When you eat these foods, the starch is broken down into sugar.

definitions.definition: Dahináanii daneeznánée dadiłdzid bits'ą́ą́dóó ha'át'íi da nihwiileehígíí biziil (energy) bits'áłdohígíí (heat) bee nida'anish. Nahasdzáán biyi'di dahólǫ́ haashį́į́ danéelt'e'go. Łeejin/tsékǫ', ak'ah łizhiní dóó níłch'i  ászólí ááh ha'ní.

translations.definition: A material formed from the decomposing organisms that can release energy as heat or be used for work. These fuels are found within the Earth and exist in limited quantities. Examples include coal, oil, and natural gas.
translations.sentence: Oil, gas, and coal are called fossil fuels because they are made from the carbon and hydrogen of living things from millions of years ago. Power plants release carbon dioxide when they burn fossil fuels, which contributes to climate change.

definitions.definition: Hinááhts'óóz ats'íís biyi'di binaanish t'ááła'ígo hólónígíí, tissues, ats'ą́ siláii ats'íís biyi'di hólǫ́ nidi doo choo'į́į dago yits'ą́ą́dę́ę́' yigáałii bits'íís hólónígíí ła' choyooł'į́ dóó binaanish hólónígíí t'áá yił aheełt'é

translations.definition: Cells, tissues, or organs in a body that have no apparent function and is similar to a functioning organ or structure in an ancestor.
translations.sentence: The wisdom teeth of our ancestors would replace any teeth that fell out or were removed.  Now, our wisdom teeth are vestigial traits because we have better mouth hygeine and dental care.

definitions.definition: Ats'íís iisiihgo ádík'ijį' naalnish yileehgo ats'íís doo bąąhtééh da yę́ę niyiiłchxǫǫh yileeh

translations.definition: Occurs when the body's immune system attacks and destroys healthy body tissue by mistake
translations.sentence: Sometimes your body's immune system mistakes your own cells as pathogens, this can cause autoimmune disease.

definitions.definition: Bik'ehgo náás oochíiłii ał'ąą ádaat'éego bee hazhdít'éhígíí naasch'ąą'go ál'įįhígíí. Dik'ą́ągo si'ą́ą łeh.

translations.definition: A square drawing used to show the different genotypes possible in the offspring when two individuals reproduce
translations.sentence: By drawing a punnet square, doctors can easily show new parents what blood types their children could have.

definitions.definition: Ats'íís bee doo bihodééłníinii ats'íís hasht'éé yoodlíiłii (protein) áyiił'įįh ats'íís atíidoolíłii yaa ákoniizįįhgo

translations.definition: A protein produced by the body's immune system when it detects harmful substances. The plural of antibody is antibodies.
translations.sentence: Antibodies are used by your body to defend itself against things like viruses, bacteria, or even cancer cells that may cause you harm.

definitions.definition: Bee iiná hodooleełii nucleotide naakigo ahidii'ash éí ats'íís bitł'óól DNA yileeh

translations.definition: Two nucleotides (also known as bases) that combine and join to make DNA. Base A combines with T and base G combines with C.
translations.sentence: DNA is shaped like a spiral staircase.  Like pieces of a puzzle, only certain base pairs can fit together to make the steps of the staircase.

definitions.definition: Ats'íís bee haleehii  naadiin ał'ąą ánéelt'e' éí ats'íís bitł'óól yee naalnishgo bee ats'íís hodooleeł dóó áadi hasht'éé yoodlííł.  Iiná bitł'óól, DNA, bee bóhólnííh éí bik'ehgo náás oochíiiłii táa'go bita'góó daníjaa'.
Ha'át'íi da náádaa łahdę́ę́' ha'át'íi da bił ahiih yilyéehgo bitsiniltł'ish bilááh éí doodago a'oh yileeh  taah ji'áahgo níłch'i hydrogen bitsiniltł'ish náyiilááh

translations.definition: The building block of protein. There are ą) amino acides, and each is specified by a DNA segment of three nucleotides (bases).
translations.sentence: Different kinds of amino acids create proteins of many shapes and functions, just as different kinds of building materials can be used to make anything from a castle to a school to a shed.

translations.definition: A substance that becomes more positively or negatively charged during a chemical reaction and accepts hydrogen ions when dissolved in water.
translations.sentence: Many toothpastes include a base like baking soda as an ingredient because it can prevent bacteria from growing in your mouth.

atsiis-bikoo.literal: ats’íís = body, bi = it, tł’óól = strand. Refers to the DNA in the cell
atsiis-bikoo.comment: need a microscope to look at it

definitions.definition: Ats'íís bitł'óól éí ats'íís bee ályaaígíí ts'ídá t'ááłá'ígo si'ánígíí náhidínísé, binaagóó yaa ákonízin, dóó ádéé'ál'į́įgo noosééł.

translations.definition: The cell is the smallest unit with the basic properties of life, such as growth, sensing the environment, and reproduction.
translations.sentence: Your body is composed of around 37 trillion cells! That is a big number but there are even more bacteria cells that live on and in you: around 100 trillion.

atsiis-bitlool.literal: ats’íís = body, bi = it, tł’óól = strand. Refers to the DNA in the cell
atsiis-bitlool.comment: refers to DNA in cell. Often used when talking about cancer

definitions.definition: Ats'íís bitł'óól éí ats'íís bee ályaaígíí ts'ídá t'ááłá'ígo si'ánígíí náhidínísé, binaagóó yaa ákonízin, dóó ádéé'ál'į́įgo noosééł.

translations.definition: The cell is the smallest unit with the basic properties of life, such as growth, sensing the environment, and reproduction.
translations.sentence: Your body is composed of around 37 trillion cells! That is a big number but there are even more bacteria cells that live on and in you: around 100 trillion.

atsiis-bitlool-altsa-dahinise.literal: ats'íís bitł'óól = cell. ałts'á = seperate. dahinisé = pulling. Meaning, a cell pulling to seperate.

definitions.definition: Ats'íís bitł'óól ałts'á níséehgo bee ats'íís bitł'óól ániidígíí náhoodleeł

translations.definition: The formation of new cells when an existing cell divides.
translations.sentence: Bacteria like E. coli reproduce by cell division, where one cell copies itself and splits into two cells.

definitions.definition: Ats'íís bitł'óól ałts'á níséego iiná bitł'óól (DNA) bik'ehgo náás oochíiłii biyi'di naaki ałch'į' naazlá, éí t'áá ahedaałt'éego ałts'ákah dóó ahidiníł náádę́ę́' niikah áádóó ałts'á nísééh.

translations.definition: The stage of cell division in mitosis or meiosis in which the doubled set of chromosomes separates into two identical groups that move to opposite ends of the cell

atsiis-bitlool-biziil.literal: ats'íís bitł'óól = cell, biziil = energy. The cell's energy.

definitions.definition: Ats'íís bitł'óól biyi'di t'áádoole'é si'ą́ągo ats'íís bitł'óól yee biinéí dooígíí bii' nihwiileeh dóó náásgóó ííł'į́

translations.definition: Principal molecule for storing and transferring energy in cells.
translations.sentence: Cells use the food you eat to make  adenosine triphosphate, which  stores chemical energy that can be used  to do work like move molecules or grow.

definitions.definition: Hináanii bits'íís naalnishígíí éí doodago na'alkaahk'ehgo dahináanii bits'íís naalnishígíí bíhoo'aah.

translations.definition: How an organism's body functions or the scientific study of how organisms function
translations.sentence: You are studying physiology when you study how blood moves through the heart or nutrients move through a plant.

atsiis-silii-yiti-doo-bineelaah-biighah.word: ats'íís silį́į' yit'į́ dóó bí'neel'ąąh bíighah

definitions.definition: Bíla'ashdla'ii hazlį́į'go ánoolnin dóó át'éegi yit'į́įgo dóó bí'neel'ąąh bihónéedzą́ągo.

translations.definition: A trait of an individual that can be observed or measured
translations.sentence: Your hair color is an example of your phenotype.

definitions.definition: Dahinánii dóó naaldeehii bits'íís silzil dóó náhiniik'áásígíí t'áá łáhági silkidgo ádayósinígíí. Binaadę́ę́' nahooniidoh dóó náhoniik'asígíí éí doo binahjį' bits'íís sizil da.

translations.definition: An organism whose internal temperature can be contantly maintained and does not depend on the temperature of their surrounding environment
translations.sentence: You are an endotherm! Endotherms often have hair or feathers to help keep warm and may sweat or pant to cool down. These adaptations help them maintain a constant temperature.

definitions.definition: T'áá ha'át'ííshį́į́ yidą́ągo bee na'nise', bee anoosééł, dóó binahjį' naanish ła' yit'ínígíí.

translations.definition: A substance that is needed for healthy growth, development, and function
translations.sentence: Foods like vegetables and meats are high in nutrients and will help you grow.  Foods like potato chips and candy provide few nutrients to help sustain your body.

definitions.definition: Atsą́siléí t'áá ał'ąą át'éego binaanish dahólónígíí.

translations.definition: A group of tissues in the body that has a specific form and function
translations.sentence: Your eyes are organs that help you see, while your heart is an organ that helps circuluate your blood throughout your body.

atsiistsin.literal: ats'íís = living. ts'ïn = bone. "bones of a living thing"

definitions.definition: Ats'íís biyi'di nitł'izgo ats'íís bá naaz'á éí ats'íís yee sizį́ dóó naha'ná

translations.definition: The internal hard structure that supports the body of a living thing
translations.sentence: Without a skeleton, our body would have no shape and we would be unable to move in a coordinated way.  We would look like a pile of organs.

definitions.definition: T'áá ha'át'íi da protons dóó electrons bináhaazléí wólta'go bitsiniltł'ish ánéelt'e dóó ábóodziilígíí

translations.definition: An object or molecule has a charge if it has an unequal number of protons and electrons.
translations.sentence: An element has a neutral charge if it has an equal number of protons and electrons. It has a +1 positive charge if it has one more proton than electron.  It has a -1 negative charge if it has one more electron than proton.

atsiniltlish-bilaah-ei-doodago-bioh-aboo.word: atsiniltł'ish bilááh éí doodago bi'oh ábóodziil

definitions.definition: Ha'át'íi da bitsiniltł'ish bilááh éí doodago a'oh yileehgo taah ji'áahgo níłch'i hydrogen bitsiniltł'ish hólǫ́ǫgo bits'á shígish.

translations.definition: A substance that becomes more positively or negatively charged during a chemical reaction and releases hydrogen ions (protons) when dissolved in water.
translations.sentence: An acid - like lemon juice or  vinegar - is useful in a marinade because it breaks down the tissue and allows flavor and oil to enter.

definitions.definition: Ats'óóz t'áá łá'í nít'i'ígíí bitsiniltł'ish bąąhgóó hólǫ́. Nerve cell ałdó' deiłní.

translations.definition: Cells that carry electrical charges through the body and are part of the nervous system. Also known as a nerve cell.
translations.sentence: Neutrons have the same weight as protons but have no charge. Together with protons, they form the nucleus of the atom and determine its weight.

definitions.definition: Ats'óóz t'áá łá'í nít'i'ígíí ats'íístahdi łą́ągo ahidadiit'i'go ats'íís yee ákonízin

translations.definition: Nerves are a collection of neurons that carry signals throughout the body
translations.sentence: Parkinson's disease is a disorder of the nervous system that occurs when neurons start to die, which affects the body's movement

definitions.definition: Atsiighąą', atsiighąą' íígháán bii'nít'i'ígíí dóó ats'óóz t'áá ałtso hináanii bits'íís yee hadít'éhígíí ats'íístahgóó da'ahi'niihgo ats'íís hiná.

translations.definition: The brain, spinal cord, and nerves of an animal that together receive messages from other parts of the body and control how the body functions
translations.sentence: Each of your fingertips has over 3,000 neurons which send signals to your brain about the things you touch or feel.

definitions.definition: Amá dóó azhé'é bik'eelyéí bee i'niitsąądgo

translations.definition: The cell that forms after gametes are joined.
translations.sentence: The zygote forms once the sperm cell enters the egg cell and they become one cell.  Identical twins are a result of a zygote splitting into two cells.

definitions.definition: Asdzání bik'eelyéí

translations.definition: The female sex cell, or gamete. Compare with sperm.
translations.sentence: In many species, females pass on their DNA to their offspring through their egg cells.

definitions.definition: Ayoonaałgo bik'ehgo náás oochíiłii łahgo ánídaa'níiłgo bik'ehgo ahii' háá' ayoochííł.

translations.definition: Changes in the frequency of alleles over generations
translations.sentence: Over the course of evolution, birds like ostriches lost the ability to fly as they evolved to survive well without flight.

definitions.definition: Hak'éí nát'ą́ą́' ahool'áago hamá dóó hazhé'é dabishchíníinii bits'ą́ą́dę́ę́' hojizhlį́į́'ígíí

translations.definition: One from whom an individual is descended, whether on the father's or mother's side, at any number of generations.
translations.sentence: Your grandfather, great-grandmother, and great-great grandmother are all your ancestors.

definitions.definition: Atom naaki éí doodago díkwíi da ahidiilyáago binitsiniltł'ish bináhaazléí (electrons) ahiidiinił

translations.definition: Two or more atoms that are joined because they share electrons
translations.sentence: Water is a molecule because it is made when two hydrogen atoms and one oxygen atom combine together.

definitions.definition: Hado bee ayoonaałgo t'áá ha'át'íi da yee hadít'éii, molecules, ahídaha'náahgo yee niidoh dóó bits'á níłdoii

translations.definition: This type of energy (also known as heat energy) is created when molecules move around quickly and randomly within a substance, which releases heat.
translations.sentence: When you boil water, the water molecules will start to move around quickly as they absorb thermal energy from the burner.

definitions.definition: Nahasdzáán bikáá' honí'ą́ą́góó ha'át'ííshį́į́ chodaa'ínígíí nihwiileehígíí doo bilááhgóó choo'į́į da

translations.definition: When a resource from the environment is not used faster than it is produced in nature.
translations.sentence: We have a symbiosis with the bacteria that live in our gut. Our stomach and intestines give these bacteria a place to live and food to eat. In turn, the bacteria help us digest our food and protect us from pathogens in our food.

definitions.definition: Ha'át'íi da bideezla' ayóo atí'áł'į́įgo ats'íís átííł'ínígíí

translations.definition: A highly reactive substance that causes obvious damage to living tissue
translations.sentence: Many household cleaners like bleach are corrosive because they can start to burn and harm your skin when left on the surface for too long.

definitions.definition: Bee adiiltłáadii t'áadoole'é ahidiilyéego bee bina'anishígíí

translations.definition: Any substance that increases the rate of a reaction without itself being consumed
translations.sentence: A catalyst makes an unlikely reaction more likely. For example, lactase is an enzyme catalyst that increases the rate that lactose, found in dairy products, is turned into two types of sugar that our bodies can use.

definitions.definition: Bik'ehgo náás oochíiłii bee ázhnoolnin dóó ájít'éhígíí áyiił'įįh hamá dóó hazhé'é bits'ą́ą́dę́ę́' bik'ehgo náás ooch'íiłii bee hojileehígíí aheełt'éego (homozygote) éí doodago doo aheełt'ée dago (heterozygote)

translations.definition: The allele that determines the phenotype when present in a homozygote or heterozygote. Compare with recessive.
translations.sentence: An individual with one allele for type-O blood and one allele for type-B blood will have type-B blood because the  allele for type-B blood is dominant to the allele for type-O blood.

bee-atsiis-haleehii-alaaji-naalnish-bee-.word: bee ats'íís haleehii alą́ąjį' naalnish bee ájít'é yileehii

definitions.definition: Ho'dizhchíídóó hojizhlį́į'go ázhnoolnin dóó ájít'éhígíí bee jinooséełii hamá dóó hazhé'é bits'ą́ą́dę́ę́' bik'ehgo náás oochíiłii alą́ąjį' (dominant) naalnishígíí bik'ehgo jinoosééł silį́į'go.  Recessive éí bik'ehgo náás oochíiłii akéédóó bik'ehgo anoosééł.

translations.definition: An inherited characteristic that appears in an offspring if it is received from a parent through a dominant allele. Compare with recessive trait.
translations.sentence: A dominant trait is determined by a dominant allele. For example, if you have an allele for light eyes and an allele for dark eyes you will have dark eyes because that allele is dominant over the allele for light eyes.

definitions.definition: Hinááhts'ózí dóó akááz éí doodago atsą́ siléí bitoo' t'áádoole'é ba'át'e' dahólóonii yich'ą́ą́h naalnishígíí

translations.definition: A collection of cells and organs that help to defend an organism against pathogens.
translations.sentence: Someone who has the flu is infectious for the first couple of days because they can give the flu to others.

definitions.definition: Ha'át'íida t'áá aaníí át'é bee bééhózinígíí nídiilyáago binahjį' baa ha'oodzíí' dóó woodlánígíí t'áá aaníigo baa nitsáhákees.

translations.definition: The available facts or information that indicate a belief or statement is true or valid

definitions.definition: T'áá ha'át'íi da dayéélta', bída'néél'ąądígíí, saad da, haalzíidgo bikáá' ályaii, dóó baa hane'go aha'ádaalyaii

translations.definition: A collection of facts, such as numbers, words, measurements, observations or descriptions of thing
translations.sentence: When you write notes about something you see, like the number of petals on a flower, you are collecting data.

definitions.definition: Hats'íís dóó ájít'éhígíí bił ho'dizhchínígíí hamá dóó hazhé'é bits'ą́ą́dę́ę́' nízhdiiláhígíí éí bik'ehgo náás oochíił'ii akéédóó naalnishígíí, recessive, bik'ehgo hojizhlį́į́'.

translations.definition: An inherited characteristic that appears in an offspring if it is received from a parent through a recessive allele. Compare with dominant trait.
translations.sentence: A recessive trait is determined by a recessive allele. For example, if you have an allele for light eyes and an allele for dark eyes you will have dark eyes because the allele for light eyes is recessive.

definitions.definition: Dahináanii t'ááłáhági haz'ą́ągo kééhat'į́įgo bee da'iináanii ałghadeidit'áahígíí

translations.definition: A negative interaction that occurs whenever two or more organisms require the same limited resource
translations.sentence: In the winter when there are few plants, the deer are in competition with one another for who gets to eat them.

definitions.definition: Hináanii bits'íístahdi t'áá ałtso ahił nidaalnishgo yee hiná dóó bits'íís bitł'óól hazhó'ó danéesé

translations.definition: In organisms, chemical reactions that help sustain life and allow cells to grow
translations.sentence: When you eat food, your body converts it into energy through a biochemical reaction called metabolism.

definitions.definition: Hinááhts'ózí bitsą́ siléí ch'iyáán néidił'eeł dóó yee bidziil dóó biinéí yileehígíí

translations.definition: An organelle that takes in nutrients, breaks them down, and creates energy (ATP) for the cell
translations.sentence: Power plants supply electricity to your home, just like the mitochondria supplies energy to the cell.

definitions.definition: Hináago naaldeeh dóó nidaat'a'ii binaagóó áhoot'éhígíí yee hadiit'įįhgo doo yit'į́į da yileeh

translations.definition: When organisms disguise their appearance to blend in with their surroundings
translations.sentence: Jackrabbits are brown so that they camouflage with the dirt and avoid being seen by predators.

definitions.definition: Hináanii t'áá bí ch'iyáán yidooyį́į́łígíí ííł' į́ t'áá ákǫ́ǫ́h binaagóó hólónígíí adinídíín dóó t'áádoole'é ałtah nihwiileehgo ahił nidaalnishígíí bits'ą́ą́ biziil choyooł'į́įgo

translations.definition: An organism capable of making its own food from inorganic substances using light or chemical energy (compare with heterotroph)
translations.sentence: You've never seen pine trees eat food because they are autotrophs and use light, water, and carbon dioxide to gain energy.

definitions.definition: Hináanii bíígháán ádaadinígíí.

translations.definition: Any animal lacking a spinal cord (also known as a backbone). Compare with vertebrate.
translations.sentence: Worms, bees, and snails are all invertebrates.

definitions.definition: Hináanii naaldeehii, animals, bits'íísts'in dóó bitsiighąą' bíígháán bii' danít'i' ígíí

translations.definition: An animal that has a spinal cord and a skeleton. Compare with invetebrate.
translations.sentence: Humans, horses, and birds are vertebrates, but an octopus has no bones or spinal cord and is thus an invertebrate.

bika-doo-biaadii-taa-geed-oochiil.word: biką' dóó bi'áadii t'áá gééd oochííł

definitions.definition: Azhé'é t'áágééd i'niitsąąh dóó ni'iichííh. Amá t'áá sáhá idínóoltsąął dóó adoołchííł.

translations.definition: The process of creating offspring without exchanging genetic information with another organism through sex.
translations.sentence: A bacteria cell can make a copy of itself by splitting into two cells.  When something reproduces without a partner, it is called asexual reproduction.

definitions.definition: T'áá ha'át'íi da át'éegi dóó naalnishígi át'éego baa hane' dóó nábinázláago binahjį' áhá'nínígíí łą́ bééhózingo

translations.definition: An explanation for why things work or how things happen that is supported by a large amount of scientific evidence
translations.sentence: Cell theory states that all organisms are made up of cells and that all new cells are produced by existing cells. We know that this is true because we can see cells under a microscope and can trace the origin of new cells back to the cells they came from. There is no evidence to suggest this theory is incorrect.

definitions.definition: Bik'ehgo náás oochíił'ii łahgo át'éhígíí.

translations.definition: A particular version of a gene.
translations.sentence: You have two copies of each gene in your genome.  If the copies are different, you have two alleles of that gene.

bikehgo-naas-oochiilii-lago-ate-siliiigi.word: bik'ehgo náás oochíiłii łago át'e silį́į́'ígíí t'áá át'é

definitions.definition: Iiná bitł'óól bąąhgi bik'ehgo jinooséełgo t'áá jizį́į́ nít'ę́ę́' ájít'é dooleełígíí

translations.definition: A section of DNA that is the unit of heredity for a trait.
translations.sentence: In cats, fur length is determined by one gene, but body size is influenced by many genes.

definitions.definition: Iiná bitł'óól łahgo ádzaa.

translations.definition: A permanent alteration in the DNA sequence
translations.sentence: Mutations are common.  Every person has a lot of mutations, but most of them are not harmful.

definitions.definition: Bíla'ashdla'ii ła' biyik'ehgo náás oołchíiłii łahgo át'é silį́į́'ígíí naakigo bee hólǫ́.

translations.definition: An individual who has two of the same alleles for a gene. Compare with heterozygote.
translations.sentence: Individuals who have two sickle cell alleles are homozygotes at that locus and will display sickle cell disease.

bikehgo-naas-oochiilii-naaki-alaa-ate.word: bik'ehgo náás oochíiłii naaki ał'ąą át'é

definitions.definition: Bíla'ashdla'ii bi'iiná bitł'óól bąąhgi bik'ehgo náás oochíiłii gene naaki ał'ąą át'éego hólónígíí

translations.definition: An individual who has two different alleles of a gene. Compare with homozygote.
translations.sentence: Individuals who only have one sickle cell allele are heterozygotes at that locus and will not display sickle cell disease.

bikehgo-naas-oochiilii-naalkaah.literal: bik'ehgo = following, náás oochíiłii = forward birth, naalkaah = study/ research. Meaning, "study the following of reproduction"

definitions.definition: Dahináanii yik'ehgo náás oołchííłígíí dóó yił oołchíiłii nidaalkaahgo t'áá át'é bíhoo'aah

translations.definition: The study of genes and inheritance in living organisms
translations.sentence: A scientist who studies corn genetics researches how traits and diseases are inherited and affect yield.

definitions.definition: T'áá hoolzhishgi dahináanii t'áá sahdii dahyikahgo yik'ehgo náás oołchíiłii łahgo át'é daazlį́į'ígíí t'áá ałtso ááł jiní

translations.definition: All of the alleles of all the genes in a population of a specific species at a given time.
translations.sentence: A species with red and orange flowers has alleles for red and orange flower color in its gene pool.

definitions.definition: Bee ájít'éii bił ho'dizhchínígíí ła' bee hadziilgo jiinánígíí hái da nááná ła' ákót'éhígíí doo bił yizhchínígíí bił ałch'į' naa'nil aheełt'éego haz'ą́ągi haalzííd.

translations.definition: A heritable trait that increases the fitness of an individual with that trait compared to individuals without that trait in a particular environment.
translations.sentence: The Jackrabbit's large ears are an adaptation that helps them hear better and stay cool in the hot desert.

bil-naas-oochiil.literal: bił náás = onward/forward. oochíił = birth. Meaning, "giving birth onward"

definitions.definition: Dahináanii yizhchíigo bimá dóó bizhé'é yik'ehgo náás oołchíiłii bee bik'i' aschínígíí yee hadít'é yileeh

translations.definition: The passing on of traits from parents to offspring through genetic information.
translations.sentence: Scientific discoveries about heredity allow scientists to identify the causes of many genetic disorders.

bil-oochiilii-bee-hazhdiidzaa.literal: bił oochíiłii = birth. hazhdiidzaa = dress up. Describing " how you look as an offspring of your parents"

definitions.definition: Hamá dóó hazhé'é bits'ą́ą́dę́ę́' bik'ehgo náás oochíiłii binahjį' yee hadít'éhígíí bił ho'dizhchį́įgo

translations.definition: To receive a particular characteristic from a parent, usually through genes that are passed from parent to offspring.
translations.sentence: When somone notices that siblings look alike, they are seeing similar traits that the siblings inherited from their parents.

bitsiniltlish-adin.literal: bitsiniltł'ish = charge. ádin = none. "Meaning, no charge"

definitions.definition: Atom yee hadít'éhígíí atsiniltł'ish bąąhgóó ádin.

translations.definition: The components of an atom that have no charge
translations.sentence: The nitrogen cycle describes the way nitrogen atoms move around the Earth.  Nitrogen atoms move around when organisms decompose, when bacteria absorb and release nitrogen, and when fertilizer used on crops washes into rivers and streams.

definitions.definition: Dahináanii bíígháán dahólónígíí  éí binaagóó áhoot'éhígíí binahjį' bits'íís sidoh dóó sik'azígíí éí (ectotherm wolyé). Yichííhgo táyi' naaleeh áádóó dahdínísééh dóó éí dóó  tábąąhdi dóó łeeshtahdi dahiná. Ch'ał ádaat'éii  dóó salamanders ááł ha'ní.

translations.definition: A vertebrate animal whose internal temperature depends on the environment (ectotherm). Most live in the water when young and live on land as adults. Examples include frogs, toads, and salamanders.
translations.sentence: Frogs are amphibians that live as tadpoles in the water when they are young, and are able to walk on land as an adult after they grow legs.

definitions.definition: Bíla'ashdla'ii dayikah nít'ę́ę́' yits'á dahanééhgo nááná łahjí dahyikahígíí yił yah adahnéehgo.

translations.definition: The movement of individuals out of a particular population or area to other populations or areas. Compare with immigration.
translations.sentence: You could say that when you leave your classroom and walk out into the hall that you are emigrating from your classroom.

definitions.definition: Nanise' ałtsaas'éí dóó ch'il shánídíín yee bidziilgo á'dólzingo hinánígíí.

translations.definition: An organism capable of photosynthesis
translations.sentence: Plants live in a variety of different environments. For example, pecan trees grow where it's warm, seaweed grow in the ocean, and piñon trees grow in or near the desert.

definitions.definition: Hináanii ch'il dóó hináanii naaldeehígíí bich'iiyą' áyósinígíí.

translations.definition: An animal that eats both plants and animals
translations.sentence: People are ominvores because we can eat both meat and vegetables.

chil-dootlizhi-doo-dahinanii-sha-yee-bii.word: ch'il dootł'izhí dóó dahinánii shá yee biinéí

definitions.definition: Ch'il, nanise', dóó dahináanii ła' shánídíín yee bidziil dóó biinéígo áyósinígíí.

translations.definition: The process by which green plants and certain other organisms turns energy from the sun into chemical energy
translations.sentence: When plants photosynthesize, they absorb carbon dioxide and release oxygen that we can breathe.

definitions.definition: Ch'iyáán ats'íís bee haleeh dóó hasht'éé yoodlííłígíí, protein, bee haleehígíí, amino acid, da'ahólzha' daaleeh dóó nidaat'ood łeh

translations.definition: A chain of amino acids that can fold into a specific shape
translations.sentence: Hemoglobin is the protein in a red blood cell that carries oxygen to different parts of your body.

definitions.definition: Ch'iyáán dahideezláádóó dahiníláajį' bee hahodít'éego nahaz'ą́. Łahgóó ahéédadiit'I'

translations.definition: Represents multiple pathways through which energy and nutrients flow through an ecosystem. It includes many intersecting food chains. It demonstrates that most organisms eat, and are eaten, by more than one species.
translations.sentence: Eagles eat mice, mice eat grain, and these three species form a food chain.

definitions.definition: Dahináanii ałkéé' bił honí'ą́ągo dahnaazhjaa'go bich'iiyą' nídanidlį́

translations.definition: A linear sequence of organisms through which nutrients and energy pass as one organism eats another
translations.sentence: A large, healthy deer that mates often will have higher fitness than a small, unhealthy deer that doesn't mate as often.

chiyaan-naalkaad-bee-jiinanigii.literal: chʼiyáán = food, bee jiinánígíí = keeping you alive... means "something about the food is keeping you alive"

definitions.definition: Łikání hadił bitahdi hólǫ́ǫgo íiyisíí bee hadziil dóó hwiinéí áhósinígíí óolyé. Łikání glucose wolyéhígíí hadił bitahdi hólǫ́ éí hats'íís ííł'į́įgo hadił bitahdi niheleeh.

translations.definition: The simple sugar that is the main source of energy. Glucose is found in the blood and is the main sugar that the body makes.
translations.sentence: Someone with diabetes needs to monitor their glucose levels, which is the amount of sugar that is in their bloodstream.

definitions.definition: Atsą́ níléí ch'iyáán dóó jidlánígíí néedił'eel dóó bitoo' ádaat'éí yee ats'íís bidziil, biinéí, dóó hats'íís háidoodlííł. Ha'át'ííshį́į́ hats'íís doo choyooł'į́į da dooígíí éí hach'íí' bee níláhgóó kót'į́

translations.definition: The organs that take in food and liquids and break them down into substances that the body can use for energy, growth, and tissue repair. Waste products the body cannot use leave the body through bowel movements
translations.sentence: Your digestive system includes your mouth, stomach, and intestines. All of these organs play a role in breaking down food you can absorb nutrients and energy.

definitions.definition: Naaldeehii bits'íístahdi ch'iyáán yididiiłił go yee bidziil dóó hináadoogo éí ch'iyáán bits'íístahdi shoogishígíí yee yidiłidígíí hodooleeł éí ch'iyáán yidiiłtláadgo bits'ą́ą́dóó biinéí dóó bidziil.

translations.definition: A substance that acts as a catalyst in living organisms, regulating the rate at which chemical reactions proceed without itself being altered in the process
translations.sentence: People that are lactose intolerant cannot make the enzyme called lactase which prevents them from digesting lactose in milk.

definitions.definition: Hináanii ch'osh bidi'nínígíí éí hastą́ą́ bijáád dóó bits'a' dahólǫ́ǫgo yee bits'íís niłdzil éí exoskeleton wolyé.

translations.definition: Small animals with six legs and a hard outer shell called an exoskeleton.
translations.sentence: A ladybug is an insect because it has six legs and an outer shell; however, a tick is not an insect because it does not have six legs.

chosh-doo-yitiinii-bee-naatseedi.literal: ch'osh doo yit'íinii = bacteria. bee naatseedí = killler. Meaning "bug spray"

definitions.definition: Ch'osh doo yit'íinii bee yigání éí doodago bee ni'óolziní

translations.definition: A substance that kills or inhibits the growth of bacteria.
translations.sentence: When harmful bacteria make you sick, the doctor may give you antibiotics to get rid of the bacteria.

definitions.definition: T'áádoole'é bits'íís bitł'óól t'ááłá'ígo yee hiná ch'osh doo yit'íinii dabijiní
Hináanii doo yit'íinii. Bee adéest'į́į́' dabidziilígíí t'éí bee daat'į́.

translations.definition: Microscopic single celled organisms.
translations.sentence: Even if your hands look clean it is important that you wash them to remove any harmful bacteria.

translations.definition: An organism too small to be seen without a microscope
translations.sentence: Sperm and egg cells are created through meiosis.

definitions.definition: Hináanii naayéé' doo bidééłníi da á'dólzin

translations.definition: The ability of an organism to withstand harmful effects
translations.sentence: Animals with thick fur have a resistance to cold temperatures.

definitions.definition: Bíla'ashdla'ii bits'íístoo' dóó hináago naaldeehii bits'íístoo' bits'íístahgóó dóó bits'íís bitł'óól biyi'góó níłch'i dóó ch'iyáán dóó yee hináanii ayiił'eeł áádóó ats'íís ałtso choyoos'įįdii níláhgóó ats'á yiile

translations.definition: Body fluid in humans and other animals that delivers necessary substances such as nutrients and oxygen to the cells and transports waste away those cells
translations.sentence: Your body will stop functioning if it loses too much blood, because blood contains the oxygen and nutrients your body needs.

definitions.definition: Bíla'ashdla'ii bidił bee bééhózindooígíí ádaalyaa. Hadił łichíí'ígíí bikáá'gónaa áhoot'éhígíí bik'ehgo háidígííshį́į́ hadił A, B, O, doodago AB.

translations.definition: A system used to group human blood into different types, based on the presence or absence of certain characteristics on the surface of red blood cells. The four main blood types are A, B, O, and AB
translations.sentence: If you have surgery, it is important to know your blood type. If you lose a lot of blood, doctors find a match for your ABO blood type so that your body accepts the donated blood.

definitions.definition: Dił litsooí

translations.definition: The non-cellular portion of blood
translations.sentence: Plasma makes up about half of our blood and has important salts, proteins, and antibodies that help fight infection.

definitions.definition: Hadił łichí'í níłch'i'

translations.definition: The blood cells that carry oxygen
translations.sentence: Anemia occurs when someone doesn't have enough red blood cells.  It can cause people to feel tired, dizzy, or short of breath.

dil-ligaai.literal: dił = blood. łigaaî = white. Meaning, white blood.

definitions.definition: Hinááhts'óóz ats'íís yee bihodééłníi da áyósin éí ii'noot'į́į́', infection, dóó ąąh dahwiidooł'aałii yich'ą́ą́h naalnish

translations.definition: Cells that are part of the body's immune system that help the body fight infection and other diseases.
translations.sentence: White blood cells are part of the immune system. There are many different types of white blood cells circulating in your blood and each type has a specific role in fighting infections.

definitions.definition: Hináanii bich'iiyą' binaagóó hólónígíí néidił'eełgo bikágí biníkáágóne'  biihishigéeshgo yee hiná.

translations.definition: Living organisms that get their nutrients by absorbing them.
translations.sentence: The green or white patches on old bread are a type of fungi called mold.

doo-hinaanii.literal: doo = no. hináanii = living. Meaning, not living

definitions.definition: Ha'át'íi da doo dahináa dago daholónígíí bee ályaa. Hináanii bits'ą́ą́dę́ę́'go éí organic át'ée doo.

translations.definition: Made of non-living materials. Compare with organic.
translations.sentence: Inorganic substances like metal and turquoise can be used to make a turquoise bracelet.

definitions.definition: Ha'át'íi da naalnishgo hoolzhish nít'ę́ę́' ni'áániił silį́į́' dóó bits'ą́ą́dóó doo łahgo áhooniił da yileeh éí náasdi hasht'e didooteeł

translations.definition: When the outcome of a process reduces further change and the process becomes stable
translations.sentence: The thermostat functions as a negative feedback loop because it keeps the temperature within a building stable by turning on the air conditioning if the temperature gets too hot or turning it off if it gets too cold.

definitions.definition: Haa'í da ha'át'íi da dahináanii t'ááłáhági bił haz'ą́ą doo nida'iiłchíigo hodeeshzhiizh

translations.definition: The situation where closely related species are unable to reproduce because of differences in location, mating time, behavior, or changes in anatomy.
translations.sentence: Two populations of squirels on opposite sides of the Grand Canyon experienced reproductive isolation for so long that they are now two different species.

doo-yitiinii-bee-nelini.literal: doo yit'íinii = what you can't see. bee nél'íní = looking at with. Meaning, "looking with at what you can't see"

definitions.definition: Bee adéest'į́į́' ha'át'íi da áłts'íísígo doo yit'į́į da nidi nitsaago daat'į́įgo ádeile'

translations.definition: A device that makes very small objects or organisms larger so that they can be observed
translations.sentence: You need a microscope to see cells dividing.

definitions.definition: Dahináanii alą́ąjį' dahóóchxǫ'góne' adahanééhígíí.

translations.definition: The species that appear first in a habitat or after a disturbance.
translations.sentence: After a forest fire, the first species to reappear is a pioneer species.

definitions.definition: T'áádoole'é haalzíidgo haa da yit'ée doogo dóó bídí'nóol'ąął bohónéedzą́ baa ha'oodzíí'ígíí

translations.definition: A testable statement that explains a set of observations.
translations.sentence: You think bees prefer sunny days over cloudy days because it is unlikely to rain on sunny days.  You test your hypothesis by comparing the number of bees visiting flowers on both sunny and cloudy days.

definitions.definition: Na'alkaahgo bééhózin silį́į'ii anáá' bee yitį́įgo haalzíídígíí.

translations.definition: The data measured or seen, often during an experiment
translations.sentence: You are making an observation when you feel a change in the weather, see a cloudy sky, or hear thunder.

definitions.definition: Ha'át'íi da t'ááłáhági át'éego hadít'é dóó t'ááłáhági át'éego naalnishígíí

translations.definition: A substance that has a constant composition and characteristic properties.
