translations.sentence: Did you know water is a chemical?  Each molecule of water contains two hydrogen atoms and one oxygen atom that bond together, which is why it is called H20.

definitions.definition: Haa da hoot'éedoo le' hasingo éí doodago na'alkaahgo naalkaahii ádoonííł nawólnínígíí

translations.definition: The chance that something will happen or of a hypothesis being correct
translations.sentence: When you role a six-sided die, the probability of rolling a three is 1/6, or 16.7%.

definitions.definition: Niyiiłkaah dóó aná'át'áahjį' hats'íís biyi'di ajiłhosh, ninájílnish dóó hasht'ez dít'é bee bíhólníihgo nijigháhígíí

translations.definition: A natural, internal process that regulates daily cycles of sleep or inactivity and wakefulness or activity
translations.sentence: The circadian rhythm of sunflowers lets them anticipate the timing and direction of the sunrise each morning so that they can face their flowers towards the sun.

definitions.definition: Nahasdzáán biyi'dę́ę́' t'áádoole'é doo dahináanii bits'ą́ą́dę́ę́' hólónígíí

translations.definition: An inorganic substance that occurs naturally
translations.sentence: Precious stones like onyx and turquiose are minerals.

hai-da-bi-allele-taa-altso.word: hái da bi allele t'áá ałtso

definitions.definition: Ha'át'íi da hináanii yik'ehgo náás oołchíiłii łahgo át'é daazlį́į́'ígíí t'áá ałtso

translations.definition: All of the alleles in a given individual's genome
translations.sentence: A sheep's genotype may include alleles for white wool and black wool, but because the black allele is dominant, the sheep's phenotype will be black wool.

definitions.definition: Haigo éí doodago ch'iyáán doo łą́ dahólǫ́ǫ da haleehgo na'at'i'í dóó naaldlooshii bits'íís ni'át'įįh, doo ayóo naha'ná da, doo ayóo sizil da,

translations.definition: A state in which an animal’s body processes are slower than usual, its body temperature falls, and its activities are limited, usually during winter or when food is scarce.
translations.sentence: Many animals that live in the mountains hibernate because there is little food in the cold winter months.

definitions.definition: Dahináanii t'ááła' dahyikahgo ts'ídá bá yá'áhoot'éehgo haz'ą́ągi yee hináanii bá hólǫ́ǫgo t'éí náás yikah

translations.definition: A species that requires specific environmental conditions or specific types of resources. Compare with generalist.
translations.sentence: Yucca plants are specialists that are only pollinated by specific species of moths. These moths are also specialists because they can only lay their eggs in yucca flowers.

definitions.definition: Hats'íístahgóó hadił nidaazlį́įgo bee níłch'i dóó ch'iyáán hats'íís bitah hólǫ́ǫgo áyósin áádóó hats'íís ałtso choyoos'įįdígíí hats'áyiiléego jiinánígíí. Kót'éego binaanishígíí éí hajéídíshjool, hats'oos łichí'í dóó hats'oos dootł'izhí.

translations.definition: The system that moves blood, oxygen, nutrients, waste, and other materials throughout the body. The circulatory system includes the heart, arteries, and veins
translations.sentence: When you have high blood pressure, your circulatory system doesn't function as well becuase your heart has to work harder to pump the same amount of blood through your body.

hinaahtsooz.literal: hinááh = life, ts'óózí = small. Means "small unit of life"

definitions.definition: Ats'íís bitł'óól éí ats'íís bee ályaaígíí ts'ídá t'ááłá'ígo si'ánígíí náhidínísé, binaagóó yaa ákonízin, dóó ádéé'ál'į́įgo noosééł.

translations.definition: The cell is the smallest unit with the basic properties of life, such as growth, sensing the environment, and reproduction.
translations.sentence: Your body is composed of around 37 trillion cells! That is a big number but there are even more bacteria cells that live on and in you: around 100 trillion.

definitions.definition: Hinááhts'ózí t'áá ahedaałt'éego dóó binaanish t'ááłá'ígo

translations.definition: A group of cells that perform a similar function
translations.sentence: Gum disease causes your gum tissue to become red, swollen, and bleed easily.  You can prevent gum disease by frequently brushing and flossing your teeth.

definitions.definition: Hinááhts'óóz ał'ąą át'éego ałts'á níséego, mitosis éí doodago meiosis, alą́ąjį' bee hahalzhííshígíí

translations.definition: The first stage of mitosis or meiosis where chromosomes are compressed

definitions.definition: Hináanii hinááhts'óóz t'ááłá'ígo bits'íís nilį́. Hináanii náádaała' éí hinááhts'óóz łą́ągo yee bits'íís dahólǫ́.

translations.definition: Organisms that consist of one cell. Compare with multicellular.
translations.sentence: Bacteria are unicellular organisms because they are made of one cell.

hinaahtsozi.literal: hinááh = life, ts'óózí = small. Means "small unit of life"

definitions.definition: Ats'íís bitł'óól éí ats'íís bee ályaaígíí ts'ídá t'ááłá'ígo si'ánígíí náhidínísé, binaagóó yaa ákonízin, dóó ádéé'ál'į́įgo noosééł.

translations.definition: The cell is the smallest unit with the basic properties of life, such as growth, sensing the environment, and reproduction.
translations.sentence: Your body is composed of around 37 trillion cells! That is a big number but there are even more bacteria cells that live on and in you: around 100 trillion.

definitions.definition: Hinááhts'ózí t'ááłá'í si'ánígíí ałts'á níséehgo naaki yileehígíí

translations.definition: When a single cell divides into two identical cells
translations.sentence: As we grow, our cells divide through mitosis to create new cells.

hinaahtsozi-altsa-niseego-iina-bitlool-a.word: hinááhts'ózí ałts'á níséego iiná bitł'óól ałkéé' niikah

definitions.definition: Hinááhts'ózí t'ááłáhádi éí doodago naakidi ałts'áníséego iiná bitł'óól ałníi'gi hasht'e' niikáahgo hodinaahígíí.

translations.definition: The time during mitosis or meiosis where copies of chromosomes line up in the center of a cell

hinaahtsozi-bijei.literal: hinááhts'ózí = cell. bijéí = heart. "Meaning, heart of the cell"

definitions.definition: Hinááhts'óóz yee naalnishgo biyi'di iiná bitł'óól ak'ah bineestiin, plasma. bik'í da'asti'go daholǫ́.

translations.definition: The part of the cell that controls the functions of the cell and where DNA is found within a plasma membrane
translations.sentence: Most cells of organisms that are eukaryotes have a nucleus in their cells.  In animals, red blood cells do not have a nucleus.

definitions.definition: Hinááts'ózí bita'góó

translations.definition: Located or occurring within a cell or cells. Compare with intercellular.
translations.sentence: If the rooms of your house are cells, the hallways between the rooms are like intercellular space.

definitions.definition: Hinááhts'óóz/ats'íís bitł'óól biyi'di si'ą́ągo bik'ésti'ii hólǫ́ áádóó t'áá ha'át'ííshį́į́ binaanish.

translations.definition: A structure within a cell that is enclosed within its own membrane and has a function
translations.sentence: Organelles in a cell are like organs in a body.  Cell organelles include the nucleus and mitochondria.

hinaahtsozi-biyi-di-bijei-binaagoo-holon.word: hinááhts'ózí biyi' di bijéí binaagóó hólónígíí
hinaahtsozi-biyi-di-bijei-binaagoo-holon.literal: hinááhts'ózí biyi' di bijéí = nuclues (heart of the cell). binaagóó = surrounding. hólónígíí = exists. Meaning, "it exists around the nuclues".

definitions.definition: Ats'íís bitł'óól biyi' hólónígíí t'áá át'é ákondi bijéí nucleus éí doo bił ólta'góó

translations.definition: All of the contents of a cell excluding the nucleus
translations.sentence: If we think of a cell as a jello salad, the cytoplasm is like the jello because it suspends and holds the contents inside.

hinaahtsozi-iina-bitlool-naaki-bee-holo.word: hinááhts'ozí iiná bitł'óól naaki bee hólǫ́
hinaahtsozi-iina-bitlool-naaki-bee-holo.literal: hinááhts'ozí = cell. iiná bitł'óól = DNA. naaki = two. bee hólǫ́ = it has. Meaning, cell with 2 DNA.

definitions.definition: Ats'íís bitł'óól éí doodago hináanii bi iiná bitł'óól ałk'í daadeesdizígíí chromosomes naaki níláago bee dahólónígíí

translations.definition: A cell or organism having two sets of chromosome
translations.sentence: Most cells in our body are diploid and have a copy of each chromosome from both of our parents.

definitions.definition: Hinááhts'óóz dínéesą́ą́dóó biyi'di iiná bitł'óól bééda'alne' silį́į'jį

translations.definition: The period of time when a cell grows and copies of DNA are made.
translations.sentence: Cells spend most of their time in interphase, before making preparations to divide.

definitions.definition: Hináanii t'áá ałhąąh daazhchí dóó danihinánígíí, Amá dóó ach'é'é éí naakidi azhchínígíí át'é

translations.definition: All of the organisms born and living at about the same time. In humans, mother and daughters represent two generations
translations.sentence: During elementary school, all the kids in your class are a part of your generation because all of you have similar ages.

definitions.definition: Hináanii

translations.definition: A living thing
translations.sentence: Animals, fungi, bacteria, and plants are all examples of organisms.

definitions.definition: Hináanii áát'įįłígíí

translations.definition: How an organism acts
translations.sentence: A spider building a web, a dog wagging its tail, or a bird calling for a mate are all examples of these organism's behavior.

definitions.definition: Haa'í da t'áá íídą́ą́' yá'át'éehgo haz'ą́ągo hináanii nááná łahdę́ę́' yikahgo yah ee'nil nít'ę́ę́' ákwe'é dahinánígíí dóó haz'ánígíí yich'į' nidahwiilná silį́įgo

translations.definition: A species that was introduced to an ecosystem that was not there before and that causes hardship to other organisms Haa
translations.sentence: Russian olive trees and buffelgrass are invasive species that outcompete native species and negatively affect of the environment.

definitions.definition: Nahasdzáán bikáá'góó hináanii kéédahat'į́įgo bił nahaz'ánígíí hináanii t'áá át'é yiłdi'ígíí

translations.definition: Any living thing that has an effect on another organism, population, community, or ecosystem
translations.sentence: Plant growth can be affected by biotic factors like herbivores and pathogens.

definitions.definition: Iiná ał'ąą ádaat'éego bits'ą́ą́dóó ayoonaałígíí éí díí dahináanii t'áá ałtso, bik'ehgo yee oołchíiłii dóó naaldeehgo bá nahaz'áą́góó dóó hózhǫ́ǫgo bá dahoo'a'gi

translations.definition: The variety of life and its processes, including the variety of living organisms, the genetic differences among them, and the communities and ecosystems in which they occur
translations.sentence: The biodiversity in a desert includes the cacti, bats, lizards, hummingbirds, and beetles that live there.

definitions.definition: Hináanii doo t'ááła' nilį́į da nidi ahíłká aná'ahinilchxéehgo

translations.definition: Association between organisms of two different species in which each benefits
translations.sentence: A mutualism occurs between people and the bacteria that live in our intestines.  The bacteria gain nutrients while they help us digest food.

definitions.definition: Dahináanii ła' ha'át'ííshį́į́ biniinaa áádįįł silį́į'go

translations.definition: The act or process of a species becoming extinct
translations.sentence: There are very few black footed ferrets still alive and they are at risk for extinction.

definitions.definition: Hináanii bił bidizhchínígíí yit'į́ yileehdi

translations.definition: A characteristic of an organism that can be observed.
translations.sentence: Hair color, eye color, and nose size are all traits you inherit from your parents.

definitions.definition: Nahasdzáán bikáá'góó áhoołts'óózígo honít'i'go iiná bináhaazlá

translations.definition: The thin zone surrounding Earth where all life exists
translations.sentence: The biosphere includes all of the places on Earth where organisms can live.

definitions.definition: Ha'át'íi da hináanii bits'íís bitł'óól t'áá ałtso bits'íís bii' hólónígíí

translations.definition: All of an organism's DNA
translations.sentence: We can compare the genomes of different people to see if we can identify which alleles cause diseases.

hinaanii-bihinaahtsozi-bijei-daholonigii.literal: hináanii = life, bihinááhts'ózí=cell, bijéí = heart (here nuclues), dahólónígíí = has. meaning "life that has cells with a nuclues"

definitions.definition: Hináanii bits'íís bitł'óól biyi'di bijéí dahólónígíí
Ha'át'íi da hinááts'óóz t'ááła'í si'ą́ągo bits'íís nilį́įgo biyi'di bijéí, nucleus doo hólǫ́ǫ da

translations.definition: Organisms whose cells contain a nucleus. Compare with prokaryote.
translations.sentence: Most of the living things you see around you, including all plants and animals, are eukaryotes.

translations.definition: A unicellular organism that does not have a nucleus and has few organelles.  Compare with eukaryote.
translations.sentence: The bacteria in your gut are prokaryotes that help you digest food and defend against pathogens.

definitions.definition: Nahasdzáán bikáa'gi haz'ą́ągo ákwe'é ha'át'ííshį́į́ dahinááshį́į́ dóó doo hináanii bee hahodít'éhígíí t'áá ałtso
Dahináanii kééhat'į́įgi áádóó ákwe'é haz'ą́ągi t'áá ałtsoní bee hahodít'éego haz'ą́

translations.definition: An environment including every living organism and non-living factor that is contained within it
translations.sentence: Deserts, rain forests, and and coral reefs are all ecosystems with distinct climates and species.

translations.definition: Place where an organism or a community of organisms lives, including all living and nonliving factors or conditions of the environment
translations.sentence: Some animal species can only survive in a specific habitat, like roadrunners that live in the deserts, brushlands, and grasslands of North America.

definitions.definition: Hináanii bits'a' dahólónígíí bits'íís bits'a' yee naazį́ dóó naaghá.

translations.definition: A hard covering on the outside of the body used for support, protection, and muscle attachment. Examples include the shells of snails and the outer covering of beetles.
translations.sentence: Scorpions have an exoskeleton that protects them from predators.

definitions.definition: Hináanii bits'ą́ą́dóó hazlį́į́'ígíí.

translations.definition: Made of or made by living organisms. Compare with inorganic.
translations.sentence: Organic substances like wood and animal hides can be used to make a drum.

definitions.definition: Ałk'idą́ą́' dahináá nít'ę́ę́'yę́ę bits'iil

translations.definition: A fossil forms over a very long time as holes left in sediment by living tissue or bones are replaced with hard minerals.
translations.sentence: Dinosaur bones are fossils and they tell us when and where dinosaurs used to live.

definitions.definition: Ha'át'íi da hináanii bidziil dóó yee hináadoo biniyé ch'il/nanise' dóó níláhgóó dahináanii yiyą́

translations.definition: An organism that eats plants or animals for energy and nutrients (compare with autotroph)
translations.sentence: People, insects, fish, and birds are examples of heterotrophs because they must eat plants and animals to survive.

definitions.definition: Hináanii ayą́ągo hiná dóó áadi ha'át'ííshį́į́ bich'iiyą' nánídlį́į́góne'

translations.definition: An organism's position along a food chain
translations.sentence: Plants are at the base of each food chain.  Animals that eat plants, like deer, are one trophic level above plants.  Animals that eat deer, like mountain lions, are one trophic level above deer.

definitions.definition: Dahináanii t'ááłá'í danilį́įgo ałhá nida'iiłchíigo noosééł

translations.definition: A group of organisms that can reproduce with one another and produce fertile offspring
translations.sentence: All dogs are the same species, but a dog and a cat are different species because they cannot mate and produce offspring together.

definitions.definition: Hináanii ła' daaztsą́ągo ch'osh doo yit'íinii dóó dláád ádaat'éii yaa tįįh yikaigo ałtso dííłdzidgo

translations.definition: The breakdown of dead organism through the action of bacteria and fungi
translations.sentence: The mold we see on old fruit is a type of fungus that decays the fruit.

definitions.definition: Ha'át'íi da hináá nít'ę́ę'go ádin silį́į́' dóó bits'íís táánoodééh dóó dííyahgo ła' łeezh nídaasdlį́į́' dóó bits'iilzéí dahólǫ́.

translations.definition: The process by which dead organic substances are broken down into simpler organic or inorganic material
translations.sentence: The leaves that fall on the ground during autum are important because they decompose and become a part of the soil, which helps other plants grow.

definitions.definition: Naałniih éí doodago ch'osh doo yit'íinii ba'át'e' hólǫ́ǫgo ats'íís biyi'di yihodiiłt'ihii.

translations.definition: A microorganism that causes, or can cause, disease or harm in the organism it lives in or on (its host)
translations.sentence: The pathogen COVID-19 affects your lungs which can give you a fever and make it hard to breathe.

definitions.definition: Ha'át'íi da hináanii nááná ła' hináanii yenoolnin, biinéé' yił aheełt'é, éí doodago yeel'į́įgo naaghá.

translations.definition: When one organism looks, sounds, or behaves like another species.
translations.sentence: Stick insects use mimicry to trick predators into thinking they are twigs.

definitions.definition: Hináanii bits'íís hinááhts'ózí łą́ągo yee hadít'éhígíí. Ła'éí hinááhts'ózí t'ááłá'ígo bits'íís nilį́.

translations.definition: Organisms that consist of more than one cell. Compare with unicellular.
translations.sentence: Horses, mushrooms, and cacti are multicellular organisms because they are made of many cells.

definitions.definition: Naaldlooshii haashį́į́ néelt'e'go íiyisíí kéyah bikáá'go da' ayą́ągo bihonéedzą́

translations.definition: Maximum population size of the species that the environment can sustain, given the food, habitat, water, and other necessities available in the environment
translations.sentence: If you use too many apps on your cell phone at once, it will slow down or stop working.  Similarly, the environment has a carrying capacity and can only support a certain number of organisms, otherwise the population size will decrease.

definitions.definition: Ha'át'íi da ałtso eeldee' éí doodago bił ahóóyáago doo ła' yidziih da silį́į́'ígíí

translations.definition: Occurs when there are no more individuals of that species alive anywhere in the world
translations.sentence: Wooly mammoths died thousands of years ago and this species is now extinct.

definitions.definition: Bíla'ashdla'ii oochííł yaa ninísą́ągo yaa hóyánígíí.

translations.definition: An organism that has reached sexual maturity.
translations.sentence: A cat is an adult once it can have kittens.

definitions.definition: Dahináanii ła' dahyikahgo łahgo ánáhoo'níiłgi kéédahat'į́ éí doodago yee dahináanii ał'ąą ádaat'éego yee kééhát'į́

Hoolzhishgo ał'ąą ánáhoo'níiłgo hináanii yídaníłdzilígíí náás yikah dóó oołchíiłgo bits'íís hadidzaaígíí ayóo yit'į́. Łahgo át'éego éí hináanii bił áhoot'éhígíí doo yíníłdzilgóó éí t'óó áádįįł dóó bits'íís doo łahgo áániił da doo.

translations.definition: A species that can thrive in many different environmental conditions or use different types of resources. Compare with specialist.
translations.sentence: A raccoon is an example of a generalist.  It lives in a variety of habitats, from cities to forests, and it eats a lot of different types of food.

translations.definition: The process where organisms with the highest fitness in an environment survive and reproduce and their phenotypes become more common in the next generation. At the same time, organisms with the lowest fitness fail to survive or reproduce less and their phenotypes become less common in the next generation.
translations.sentence: Natural selection will favor desert mice with light fur because they are less likely to be seen by predators.

definitions.definition: Dahináanii shánídíín choyooł'į́įgo yee biinéí dóó bidziil dahólǫ́ éí ts'ídá binahjį' ch'iyáán nihwiileeh

translations.definition: Organisms that are capable of photosynthesis and are the basis of a food chain. Also called primary producer.
translations.sentence: A common food chain in a field includes grass, which are producers that can be eaten by grasshoppers, which then can be eaten by other animals.

definitions.definition: Hináanii biyázhí yishchínígíí yisdádoogáałgo bidziilgo hináadoo

translations.definition: The ability of an individual to successfully produce offspring compared to other individuals of the same species.
translations.sentence: In many species, organisms pass on their DNA to their offspring when an egg and sperm cell combine during fertilization.

definitions.definition: Kéyah bikáá'góó áłch'į́į́dígo ninádahałtį́įhgi

translations.definition: Barren area of land where little precipitation occurs
translations.sentence: The Sonoran Desert is found in the southwestern United States and the species that live in it can survive long periods of time with little to no rain.

definitions.definition: Hináanii biką'ígíí bik'eelyéí. Dabi'áádígíí ałdó' bik'eelyéí hólǫ́, biyeezhii (egg) deiłní.

translations.definition: The male sex cell, or gamete in animals.  Compare with egg.
translations.sentence: Car accidents are the leading cause of injuries to the spinal cord. If damaged, the spinal cord cannot repair itself.  This is why it is important to wear your seatbelt.

definitions.definition: Nahasdzáán bikáagi haashį́į́ honíłtso haz'ą́ągo nanise' dóó naaldeehii bá yá'áhoot'éehgo bił hoolzhish

translations.definition: A regional or global land area that is characterized by the plants, animals and climate in that area
translations.sentence: A desert is a biome that has plants and animals that live successfully with little or no rainfall.

iina-bitlool.literal: iiná = life, bitł'óól = strands. meaning "strands of life"

definitions.definition: Ats'íís bitł'óól nineezgo hats'íís biyi'di hólónígíí bik'ehgo náás oochíiłii yee hadít'é. Bik'ehgo ch'iyáán ats'íís ánáyoodlíiłii proteins hats'íístahgóó hólónígíí nihwiileeh.

translations.definition: A long molecule that contains our unique genetic information. It holds the instructions for making all of the proteins in our bodies.
translations.sentence: Construction workers need instructions to know how to build a building.  Cells use DNA as instructions to tell them what to do and how to make things.

iina-bitlool-adeealneeh.literal: iiná bitł'óól = DNA. ádéé'alnééh = copy. Meaning, "DNA copying itself"

definitions.definition: Ats'íís bitł'óól ałts'á hiniséego iiná bitł'óól biyi'di dahólónígíí bééda'alne'

translations.definition: The process by which DNA is copied during cell division
translations.sentence: In humans, DNA is about 6 billion base pairs long. DNA needs to be copied accurately each time a cell divides. Sometimes there can be an error in the DNA replication process. This is one way mutations can arise.

definitions.definition: Iiná bitł'óól, DNA dóó RNA t'áá áłah ałkéé' danéedééhígíí, ats'íís bee haleehii proteins, éí doodago bik'ehgo ázhnoolnin dóó ájít'é yileehígíí ahedaałt'éego

translations.definition: Similarity of DNA or RNA sequences, proteins, or traits due to shared ancestry
translations.sentence: Bees, ants, and wasps share a homologous trait of three-part bodies (abdomen, thorax, and head) because they have shared ancestry.

definitions.definition: Iiná bitł'óól, DNA dóó RNA bee haleehígíí (base deiłníigo hináanii bee hodooleełígíí)

translations.definition: The building blocks of the RNA and DNA, also known as 'bases'. The symbols for the four bases are A, T, G, and C.
translations.sentence: The nucleus of an oxygen atom contains 8 protons and 8 neutrons.

iina-bitlool-bitoh.literal: iiná bitł'óól = DNA. bit'oh = nest. Meaning, Nests of DNA

definitions.definition: Iiná bitł'óól nídahineeshzhee'go ats'íís bitł'óól biyi'di dahólónígíí

translations.definition: A structure made up of DNA tightly coiled many times around proteins
translations.sentence: Humans have two copies of 23 chromosomes.  People who have an extra copy of one or part of their 21st chromosome are born with a condition called Down Syndrome.

definitions.definition: Bik'ehgo náás oochíiłii ałkéé' sinilyę́ę łahgo ádzaago ba'át'e' hazlį́į'go bits'ą́ą́dóó ąąh dahaz'ą́ bił oochííł yileeh.

translations.definition: A disease that is caused by a change (mutation) in an individual's DNA sequence
translations.sentence: A disease like xeroderma pigmentosum makes you sensitive to sunlight and is a genetic disorder because it can be inherited from your parents.

definitions.definition: Ha'át'íi da hináanii bits'íís dóó bíni' da yee hadít'éhígíí t'áá ákólnéehgo be'iiná bitł'óól łahgo ál'įįhígíí

translations.definition: The deliberate modification of the characteristics of an organism by changing its DNA or RNA
translations.sentence: There are types of corn that have had their DNA genetically engineered so that they can resist insect herbivores.  Organisms that have had their DNA engineered are called genetically modified organisms (GMOs).

iina-bitlool-taa-lai.literal: iiná bitł'óól = DNA. t'áá łá'í = one. Meaning, "One DNA".

definitions.definition: Hinááts'ózí éí doodago hináanii bits'íístahdi iiná bitł'óól ałk'ídeesdizígíí chromosomes t'ááłáhájí t'éí biyi'di hólǫ́

translations.definition: A cell or organism having a single set of chromosomes
translations.sentence: Unlike most cells in our body, egg and sperm cells are haploid and have one copy of each chromosome.

definitions.definition: Bik'ehgo náás oochíiłii iiná bitł'óól ałk'ídeesdizígíí, chromosome, bee at'ééd éí doodago ashkii jileehígíí bąąhgi hólónígíí

translations.definition: A gene that is located on a sex chromosome
translations.sentence: Humans have two sex chromosomes called X and Y.  The genes that cause red-green color blindness are sex-linked genes because are passed down on the X chromosome.  Males inherit only one X chromosome, and all alleles on that chromosome act as dominant alleles.  This is why red-green color blindness is more common in men.

iina-naalkaah.literal: iiná = life, naalkaah = study. means to study life.

definitions.definition: Dahináanii t'áá ałtso baa nahodigį'go naalkaah

translations.definition: The scientific study of life and living organisms
translations.sentence: In biology class, you learn how all of life functions and changes over time, from the smallest celled organisms to vast ecosystems.

definitions.definition: T'áádoole'é bááhádzidii tł'óó'di nahaz'ą́ą́góó yahiigeehgo. Díí bááhádzidii pollutants wolyé.

translations.definition: The introduction of harmful materials into the environment. These harmful materials are called pollutants.
translations.sentence: Car exhaust is a type of pollution that can cause lung damage when animals breathe it in.

keedilyeeh.comment: this applies to all types of vegetation. Word will change If start talking about specific planting of something ie "plant corn, kil dilyeeh"

definitions.definition: Dá'ák'eh, k'eelyéí, daneest'áanii, dóó iiná naalyé bíhoo'ą́ą́'

translations.definition: The art, science, or practice of cultivating the soil, growing crops, and raising livestock
translations.sentence: Successful agriculture depends on farmers knowing the right time of year to plant their seeds, the correct soil, and how much water their plants need to grow.

definitions.definition: Asdzání bik'eelyéí

translations.definition: The female sex cell, or gamete. Compare with sperm.

definitions.definition: Bíla'ashdla'ii kéédahat'į́įgi yah ahekááh dóó ch'éédahakááh.

translations.definition: The movement of individuals in and out of a population or area
translations.sentence: To find food, geese migrate to the north during the summer and migrate back to the south during the winter.

definitions.definition: Dahináanii dóó naaldeehii bá haz'ą́ągi níłch'I, tó, dóó kéyah yee dahinánígíí

translations.definition: The air, water, and land in or on which all living organisms live
translations.sentence: Cacti can survive in hot and dry environments because they are good at storing and conserving water.

definitions.definition: Haa'í da diné kééhat'į́įgo daashį́į́ néelt'e' ákwe'é kééhát'į́ éí daashį́į́ néelt'e' haashį́į́ nízahjį' nihoolzhiizhgo daneeznánígíí/bikéé' ádahasdįįd yéélta'ígíí ałch'į' sinilgo baa hane'

translations.definition: The number of total deaths in comparison to the total population size in a specified community or area over a specified period of time
translations.sentence: If 20 cows die on a farm with 100 total cows, then we could say that 0.2 cows die for every cow on the farm (20 cows / 100 cows = 0.2 cows).

definitions.definition: Ch'iyáán íiyisíí bee ats'íís bidziil dóó biinéí áyósinígíí. Łikání t'éí át'é.

translations.definition: An important nutrient used as energy by the body and made mostly of sugars
translations.sentence: Your body can use the carbohydrates  in foods like bread and potatoes  as sources of energy.

definitions.definition: Nanise' ałtaas'éí łikání bii' hólónígíí

translations.definition: The main form of stored carbohydrates in plants.
translations.sentence: Traditional buffalo hunting was once a sustainable hunting method because hunting buffalo did not negatively affect their population.

definitions.definition: Ats'íís biyi'di siláhígíí ats'íís ałtso choyoos'įįdígíí níláhgóó yits'ą́ąjį' kwííł'ínígíí

translations.definition: The organs that remove waste from the body.
translations.sentence: The urinary system is the filter of the bloodstream as it captures waste and releases it from the body.

definitions.definition: Hináanii bits'íís bitł'óól (cells) daholónígíí biyi'di bijéí (nucleus) iiná bitł'óól dóó bik'ehgo náás oochíiłii nídahineeshzhee'.

translations.definition: A large group of multicellular eukaryotic organisms.
translations.sentence: People, butterflies, fish, and cats are all examples of animals.

definitions.definition: Hináanii ał'ąą ádaat'éhígíí bił haz'ą́ągi ahił dahi'ná dóó binaagóó áhoot'éhígíí chodayooł'ínígíí

translations.definition: The study of how organisms interact with one another and with their environment
translations.sentence: Emissions from coal plants can affect the ecology of an ecosystem by polluting the environment and affecting how organisms survive and interact with each other.

naaldeehii-daalghaligii.literal: naaldeehii = animals; daʼalghałígíí = eaters or butchers.

definitions.definition: Hináanii naaldeehii naaldeehii deiyánígíí

translations.definition: Animals that eat other animals
translations.sentence: Mountain lions have very sharp teeth because they are carnivores and need to tear apart the animals they eat.

naaldeehii-naalkaah.literal: naaldeehii = animal. naalkaah = study. Meaning, studying animals

definitions.definition: Hináanii naaldeehii, animals, ał'ąą át'éego dahyikahígíí haalzííd dóó naalkaahgo bíhoo'aah

translations.definition: The scientific study of animals
translations.sentence: Ranchers use their zoology knowledge to breed sheep so their offspring have more meat or fluffier wool.

definitions.definition: Hináanii naaldeehii binaagóó áhoot'éhígíí bik'ehgo bits'íístahdi sizil dóó náhiniik' áás

translations.definition: An organism whose internal temperature depends on the temperature of their surrounding environment
translations.sentence: Ectotherms, like lizards, can't control their body temperature and often lay in the sun to warm up.

naaldlooshii.literal: living things that trot or walk

definitions.definition: Hináanii bits'íís bitł'óól (cells) daholónígíí biyi'di bijéí (nucleus) iiná bitł'óól dóó bik'ehgo náás oochíiłii nídahineeshzhee'.

translations.definition: A large group of multicellular eukaryotic organisms.
translations.sentence: People, butterflies, fish, and cats are all examples of animals.

definitions.definition: Na'at'i'í éí doodago naaldlooshii chil deiłchozhígíí

translations.definition: Animals that eat plants
translations.sentence: Rabbits are herbivores that only eat leaves, seeds, twigs, and other parts of plants.

definitions.definition: Na'alkaah bee éé'deetánígíí náás hideesnilgo íhoo'aah bínááhiniidééh

translations.definition: The creation of new knowledge or the use of existing knowledge in a new way so as to create new concepts, methods, and understandings
translations.sentence: Decades of scientific research on vaccine ingredients and side effects has shown that vaccines do not cause autism.

definitions.definition: Ałkéé' honí'ą́ągo na'alkaah bee doo naaki nilį́į́góó éé'deetį́į́h dóó íhoo'aah. I'diił'áii bééhózingo ál'įįh, át'éedoo dóó nídleeł baa nitáháskéezgo saad ál'įįh, díí baa nitsáháskéézígíí naalkaah,

translations.definition: A way for scientists to study and learn things. In the first step, a problem is identified, then a hypothesis is made from existing information, and then the hypothesis is tested with an experiment.
translations.sentence: Scientists have hypothesized that plants can absorb metal contamination. Following the scientific method, they conducted an experiment to test their hypothesis and observed that metal contaminants can be found in plant tissues.

definitions.definition: Haa'í da haz'ą́ągo ákone' éé'deetįįh biniyé nida'alkaah, nida'nitin, éí doodago azee' dóó t'áá ha'át'ííshį́į́ béésh da, tó da, níłch'i da ałtah ádaalne'go bee t'áadoole'é ádaalne'

translations.definition: A place for science experiments, teaching, or the making of medicine or chemicals
translations.sentence: Any space set aside for performing science experiments is a laboratory. This could even include your own backyard.

naalkaahi.literal: Naʼałkaahíí = thing that does the investigating "Reseacher" . naʼałkaah = research, the íí is what makes it describing.

definitions.definition: Na'alkaah yeda'aniihgo éé'deetįįh yinidaalnish dóó t'áá ha'át'ííshį́į́ hiná éí doodago naalnishgi át'áo yéé'dítį́į́h. Hada'asííd, ída'neeł'ąąh, dóó bił bééhoozinígíí yaa dahalne'.

translations.definition: People who discover new things and research how things work. They observe, measure, and communicate results
translations.sentence: Not all scientists work in a lab.  Anyone who uses observations and research to answer a question is a scientist. That can even include you!

definitions.definition: Hináanii doo yit'į́į dago nááná łahdę́ę́' yigáałgo ats'íís bihinááhts'óóz biyi'di hiná silį́į́', dóó áadi hinááhts'óóz yee binahjį' noosééł yileeh áádóó bits'ą́ą́dóó ąąh dahaz'ą́ haleeh

translations.definition: A small parasite that lives within the cells of other organisms, cannot reproduce without the help of the cell it lives inside of, and causes disease.
translations.sentence: Hantavirus is a virus that causes serious lung disease. You can catch hantavirus from the saliva, urine, or droppings of deer mice and other rodents.

definitions.definition: Ba'át'e' hólóonii (pathogen) bits'ą́ą́dóó ii'noot'į́į́' doodago łóód haleehgo doo ákójít'ée da jileehígíí: naałniih (virus), ch'osh doo yit'íinii, éí doodago yaa' ádaat'éii.

translations.definition: An illness caused by a pathogen, such as a virus, bacteria, or parasite.
translations.sentence: Our immune system is made up of white blood cells and tissues like our skin which can protect us from bacterial and viral infections

definitions.definition: Dahináanii hináanii náánáła' neiłtseed dóó yiyą́ąyee hiná

translations.definition: An organism that lives mostly by killing and eating other animals
translations.sentence: Mountain lions are predators because they must hunt other animals to survive.

definitions.definition: Hináanii dahináanii nááná ła' neitseed dóó yiyą́ągo.

translations.definition: The killing and eating of one organism (the prey) by another (the predator)
translations.sentence: Predation occurs when a lizard eats a fly, a cat eats a mouse, or a bear eats a fish.

definitions.definition: Náás oochííł, bits'ą́ą́dę́ę́' oodáał ii naaltsoos bikáá' naashch'ąą'

translations.definition: A drawing that shows how ancestors and descendents are related
translations.sentence: You can track the pedigree of a horse if you know who the parents and grandparents were.  This information lets you keep your horses from interbreeding.

definitions.definition: Nahasdzáán bikáá'góó sidooígíí de'áániił. Níłch'i hado yił hidideełígíí, greenhouse gases, łą́ dahshoogish silį́į́'ígíí biniinaa nahasdzáán ayóo sido silį́į́'.

translations.definition: The increase in the Earth's average temperature. Recently, temperatures are rising rapidly due to increases in the levels of greenhouse gases.
translations.sentence: One of the results of burning fossil fuels is global warming, which is causing a rise in the average temperature of the planet.

definitions.definition: Níłch'i nitrogen, t'eesh carbon dóó t'áádoole'é doo dahináanii bits'ą́ą́dę́ę́' dahólónígíí inorganic substances bił ááhoo'níiłgo bił hoogáałígíí hináanii nídanidlį́ dóó nahasdzáán bikáá'góó ał'ąą nídadilzhish

translations.definition: The cycle in which nitrogen, carbon, and other inorganic elements of the environment are converted into the organic substances of animals or plants and released back into the environment
translations.sentence: The water cycle is a biogeochemical cycle that describes the way water moves around the Earth.  It includes clouds forming, rain falling, plants absorbing water from the soil, and water evaporating from rivers and lakes.

definitions.definition: Nahasdzáán ninálkaahgo ha'át'íi da yee hadít'éhígíí, biyi'di dóó bikáá'góó hólónígíí dahazlį́į́'dę́ę́' dóó yee naalnishígíí bíhoo'aah

translations.definition: The science that deals with the earth's physical structure, the materials it is made of, its history, and the processes that act on it.
translations.sentence: A scientist who studies geology can research how structures like mountains and canyons were formed.

definitions.definition: Chil dóó nanise' ał'ąą ádaat'éii naalkaah

translations.definition: The scientific study of plants
translations.sentence: You are studying botany when you learn about how plants function, grow, and reproduce.

definitions.definition: Chil nanise', ch'osh doo yit'íinii dóó naaldeehii danilínígíí  yee nanise' dóó nanit'ąígíí bikágí.

translations.definition: A structural layer surrounding cells of plants, bacteria, and other non-animal cells, found just outside the cell membrane
translations.sentence: Penicillin is an antiobitic that can kill bacteria by stopping them from making cell walls.  This is why doctors prescribe it when you have an infection.

definitions.definition: Ats'íís bitł'óól yee biinéí dóó hinánígíí yee ádinaalnishígíí

translations.definition: The process through which cells convert sugars into ATP (energy)
translations.sentence: Cellular respiration takes place in the mitochondria and it produces the energy needed for cells to function properly.

nanise-bitoo-dootlizhi.literal: nanise' = refers to vegatative growth, bitoo' = juice, dootł'izhí = green. "The green juice of the plant"

definitions.definition: Nanise' yee dadootł'izhígíí adinídíín (shánídíín) łahgo áyiił'įįhgo bee bitoo' naalnish yileehgo biinéí

translations.definition: Any green molecule that converts light energy into chemical energy during photosynthesis
translations.sentence: Plants use different colored pigments, such as chlorophyll, to absorb light and turn it into chemical energy, just as solar panels absorb sunlight and convert it to electricity.

nanise-biziil-iilini.literal: nanise' = refers to vegatative growth, biziil = strength, ííł'íní = making it. meaning "The growing things streangth is made"

definitions.definition: Nanise' bihiináts'ózí biyi'di si'ą́ągo adinídíín yee binéí áyosinígíí

translations.definition: A cell organelle that contains chlorophyll, which produces energy through photosynthesis
translations.sentence: If we think of chlorophyll as the solar panels of the plant cell, then chloroplasts are like a field that contains many solar panels.

definitions.definition: Na'alkaahgo bééhózin silį́į'ii anáá' bee yitį́įgo haalzíídígíí.

translations.definition: The data measured or seen, often during an experiment

definitions.definition: Dahináanii nááná łahdę́ę́' hináanii nida'ałtseedii chodabooł'ínígíí

translations.definition: An organism that is hunted or caught for food, usually by a predator.
translations.sentence: Deer are prey for many animals including mountain lions and people.

definitions.definition: Bíla'ashdla'ii kééhat'į́įgi t'áá hoolzhishgóne' t'áá át'é yéélta'go dóó éí hoolzhishígíí haashį́į́ néelą́ą́' ániid náádaazhchínígíí yéélta'go ałhąą naa'nil.

translations.definition: The number of total live births in comparison to the total population size in a specified community or area over a specified period of time
translations.sentence: If 20 calves are born on a farm with 100 total cows, then we could say that 0.2 calves are born for every cow on the farm (20 calves / 100 cows = 0.2 calves per cow).

definitions.definition: Nahasdzáán bikáá'góó dóó hót'ááhgóó áhoot'éhígíí hado dóó hak'az, níłtsą́, dóó nídaadzasígíí łahgo áániił. T'áá bí éí łahgo ánáánííł nít'ę́ę́' k'adgo níłch'i carbon dioxide wolyéhígíí bilááh ánéelt'e' silį́į́' ałk'idą́ą́' dahináanii bik'éé dahwiilzhiizhii bits'ą́ą́dóó kǫ' choo'į́įgo biniinaa níłch'i ał'ąą ánáá'nííł łahgo áyiilaa.

translations.definition: The change in global or regional weather patterns over time.  This occured naturally over time, but recently increased levels of carbon dioxide due to the use of fossil fuels from the mid to late ą)th century onwards has caused climate change to occur faster.
translations.sentence: Climate change is causing weather extremes, such as increasing temperatures and making droughts more common in the southwest United States.

definitions.definition: Níłch'i hót'ááhgóó hólónígíí

translations.definition: A chemical element that is essential for life on earth
translations.sentence: Oxygen is a part of the air that we breathe and the water that we drink.

definitions.definition: Níłch'I carbon dioxide wolyéhígíí hót'ááhgóó dóó níłch'I bitahgóó, dahináanii bitahgóó dóó táyi'góó hoo'nááłgo ahééhwiilzhíísh

translations.definition: The movement of carbon dioxide between the atmosphere, living organisms, and land and acquatic ecosystems.
translations.sentence: The carbon cycle describes the way carbon atoms move around the Earth.  Carbon atoms move around when animals decompose, plants grow, fossil fuels are burned, and oceans absorb carbon dioxide (CO2).

definitions.definition: Níłch'i nahasdzáán bikáá'góó náhoniidohígíí yił hidideeł dóó doo yiidichxíid dago biniinaa doo nahasdzáán bits'ą́ąjį' aheldóoh da.

translations.definition: Any gas that can absorb heat from Earth's surface and keeps it from leaving the atmosphere into space.
translations.sentence: Greenhouse gases act like a blanket around Earth, trapping heat instead of letting it escape from the atmosphere.

definitions.definition: Atsą́ siléí internal organs ła' ats'íís biyi'di dóó ats'íís binaadę́ę́' níłch'I ałnánáálį́įgo yee biinéí dóó bidziilgo ábósin

translations.definition: The group of organs involved in the exchange of gas between an organism and the environment to make energy (respiration).
translations.sentence: In vertebrates, the lungs are an important part of respiratory system as they bring oxygen into the body when we breathe in, and release carbon dioxide when we breathe out.

definitions.definition: Níłch'i t'óó ahayóijį' t'áádoole'é dabitah nahasdzáán bikáagi, nahasdzáán biyi'góó, hót'áahdi níłch'i biyi'góó, dóó dahinánígíí bitahgóó ahéénílį́.

translations.definition: The movement of nitrogen through living organisms, the atmosphere, and the ground.
translations.sentence: The order of nucleotides in a strand of DNA and RNA determines the protein produced.

definitions.definition: T'áá ha'át'íi da bizhool molecules łą́ hólónídóó áłch'į́į́dí hólǫ́ǫjį' ashiigéeshgo

translations.definition: The movement of molecules from an areas where there are many to where there are fewer
translations.sentence: As you add tea leaves to hot water, the tea will start to spread through the water.  This is an example of diffusion.

definitions.definition: Bee idí'nóotsąął amá dóó azhé'é bits'ą́ą́dę́ę́' ahidii'ashgo bee t'áá sahdii ázhnoolningo dóó ájít'éego hojileehígíí

translations.definition: The combination of different gametes to form unique offspring that are genetically different from their parents.
translations.sentence: When a male and a female lizard mate, they produce offspring by sexual reproduction.

oochiil-ba-silaii.literal: oochííł = reprodutive. bá siláii = laid down. Meaning "reproductive tract"

definitions.definition: Ats'íís biyi'di bee idínótsąął dóó adoochííł biniyé dahólónígíí

translations.definition: The group of organs involved in reproduction
translations.sentence: The reason that female animals get pregnant is because their reporductive system has specialized organs for carrying offspring.

definitions.definition: Ootsą́; I'niitsąąd dóó awéé' hazlį́į'jį'

translations.definition: The period of time in which an unborn offspring is carried within the body.
translations.sentence: In African elephants, pregnancy can last up to 22 months while pregnancy in cats is about 9 weeks.

definitions.definition: Shá bits'ą́ą́dę́ę́' deesdoiígíí hináanii bił nahaz'ą́ądi dóó biyi'di hasht'e dit'eehígíí.

translations.definition: A picture that represents how much energy, initially from the sun, is retained or stored in living organisms at each trophic level in an ecosystem

definitions.definition: Shá, jį́honaa'éí, bits'ą́ą́dę́ę́' adinídíín, bits'áníłdoii, dóó bits'áshígishii

translations.definition: Light, heat, and other kinds of energy given off by the Sun
translations.sentence: The function of solar panels are to capture solar energy and convert it to a usable electricity.

definitions.definition: Ha'át'íi da naalnishgo hoolzhish nít'ę́ę́' ni'áániił silį́į́' dóó bits'ą́ą́dóó doo łahgo áhooniił da yileeh éí náasdi hasht'e didooteeł

translations.definition: When the outcome of a process reduces further change and the process becomes stable
translations.sentence: When you get near a hot stove, your skin sends signals to your brain through your nerves to warn you to move away.

definitions.definition: Hináanii t'ááła' dahyikahígíí t'ááłá'ígo

translations.definition: A single, separate organism distinguished from others of the same kind
translations.sentence: Type II diabetes is a result of being unable to regulate blood sugar levels because the body doesn't respond to a hormone called insulin correctly.

definitions.definition: Hináanii ła' dahyikah nít'ę́ę́' t'áa hodina'í noonééł

translations.definition: Extinction of a large number of species within a short period of time
translations.sentence: Mammals live in a variety of different environments. For example, horses run on land, whales swim in the ocean, and bats fly through the sky.

definitions.definition: Hinááts'ózí cells binaagóó ha'át'íi da doo hináanii bits'ą́ą́dę́ę́' dahólónígíí yee biinéí doo bidziil dooígíí t'áá bí ííł'į́

translations.definition: Cells that create their own energy and biological materials from inorganic chemicals
translations.sentence: Most chemotrophs live in harsh environments like hot spring pools or near thermal vents at the bottom of the ocean.

definitions.definition: T'áá ha'át'ííshį́į́ baa ajółta' éí doodago jinéł'į́įgo nijiłkaah t'áá nináníłtsojį' éí bee béézhdi'dootį́į́ł biniyé.

translations.definition: To study or examine something in detail, in order to discover more about it
translations.sentence: When you are confused about an object or idea and you want to know how it works, you will analyze it to better understand it.

definitions.definition: T'áádoole'é yee bidziilii yee ałch'į' bótá'ii. Díí ałtsá'niłgo yee bidziilii áyóó' át'éego sidogo bits'áshígish

translations.definition: This type of energy is what holds atoms of a molecule or molecules of a substance together. When these groups of atoms or molecules are separated during a chemical reaction, chemical energy is released, often as heat.  When organisms photosynthesize or digest food, chemical reactions provide chemical energy for their growth and development.
translations.sentence: The battery in your car or phone uses checmical energy from the battery to function.

definitions.definition: Ha'átíi da álnéehgo bil hoolzhishgo álnééhígíí náás áániił éí doodago ni'áániił silį́į́'

translations.definition: When the outcome of a process causes further change and the process increases or decreases
translations.sentence: Climate change is accelerating because of a positive feedback loop.  Increased carbon dioxide concentrations cause global temperatures to rise. As oceans warm, they can't absorb as much carbon dioxide, which further warms the climate.

definitions.definition: Ha'át'íi da t'ááła' bizhi'go t'áá náó'nííłgo bits'ą́ą́dóó t'áá ha'át'ííshį́į́ náádleeł doo áko bee ályaii yee da'áhótą'ígíí ałts'á há'nííł éí doodago łahgo át'éego ádaalne'

translations.definition: Any process in which substances combine or are broken down into other substances when chemical bonds between atoms are formed or broken
translations.sentence: Jewlery that is made of nickel and silver can produce a chemical reaction with oxygen that will turn your skin green.

definitions.definition: Ha'át'íi da t'áá aaníí át'é dóó ó'ooł'įįłgi nabíhonitaahgo naalkaah.

translations.definition: A test done in order to learn something or to discover whether something works or is true.
translations.sentence: If you have dipped your toe into water  to see if it was warm enough for you  to swim, then you have conducted an  experiment!

definitions.definition: Níláhgóó ał'ąą ánáhoo'níił nidi ats'íístah di doodago hinááhts'óóz/ats'íís bitł'óól biyi'di t'ááłáhági áhoot'é.

translations.definition: The ability of the body or a cell to maintain a stable internal environment despite changes in external conditions.
translations.sentence: You sweat when it is hot and shiver when it is cold because of homeostasis. This is your body's way of staying at a constant temperature.

definitions.definition: T'ááłáhági haz'ą́ągi bíla'ashdla'ii éí doodago dahináanii t'áá ahedaałt'éhígíí kééhat'į́ dóó da'ahéelchííh

translations.definition: A group of individuals of the same species living and reproducing within a given area
translations.sentence: The people in your town, the pine trees in a forest, and the trout in a lake are examples of populations.

definitions.definition: Dahináanii kééhat'į́įgi haadahoníłtsogo hahoodzogo bii' haashį́į́ néelt'e' naaldeehígíí.

translations.definition: The number of individuals of the same species living within a given area
translations.sentence: Albuquerque has a higher population density than Window Rock.  About 3,000 people live in each square mile within Albuquerque while Window Rock has 589 people per square mile.

definitions.definition: T'áá łáhági haz'ą́ągi ha'át'ííshį́į́ dahinááshį́į́ ahił nidaalnish dahinánígíí

translations.definition: Interacting group of various species in a common location
translations.sentence: Hummingbirds and fields of flowers are in the same community because the hummingbirds need the flowers for nectar and the flowers need hummingbirds to spread their pollen.

tatlid.literal: łʼid = smoke , tá = under = under water smoke type of thing/describing the hazyness of how it looks.

definitions.definition: Tátł'id éí táyi' éí doodago hoditłéé'góó dahólǫ́ bits'íís bitł'óól t'áá łá'í éí doodago łą́ áádóó dootł'izh łeh éí doodago t'áá łahgo ánoolnin łeh.

translations.definition: Unicellular or multicellular organisms occurring in fresh or salt water or moist ground, that have chlorophyll and other pigments but lack true stems, roots, and leaves
translations.sentence: Algae can grow as single cells or large green clumps and sometimes appear to cover the entire water surface if enough of them grow. They are similar to plants because they also use the sun to make their own food.

definitions.definition: Tó nahasdzáán bikáá'góó dóó biyi' hólǫ́  dóó hót'ááhgóó dahólǫ́ éí hoo'nááł dóó danílínígíí bił ahééhwiizhíísh

translations.definition: The movement of water on, above, or below the surface of our planet.
translations.sentence: The water cycle describes how water moves around the earth.  Water moves around when it rains, when rivers transport it long distances, when it evaporates from the surface of leaves, or when ocean currents move it around the globe.

definitions.definition: Ha'át'íi da bizhool,  molecules, éí doodago bikáá'góó tó bił ałtah yileehígíí

translations.definition: Molecules and surfaces that do interact with water
translations.sentence: Salt is hydrophilic; it is easily dissolved when it is mixed with water.

definitions.definition: Ha'át'íi da bizhool,  molecules, éí doodago bikáá'góó tó doo bił ałtah yileehígíí

translations.definition: Molecules and surfaces that do no interact with water
translations.sentence: Oil is hydrophobic; it will not dissolve when mixed with water.

definitions.definition: T'áádoole'é hináago nááná łahjį' hináanii choyooł'į́įgo yee hinánígíí.

translations.definition: An organism that lives on or in another organism (its host).
translations.sentence: You could get a parasite like a tapeworm in your gut when you eat undercooked meat from infected animals.

definitions.definition: Hináanii nááná ła' choo'į́įgo bee jiináago.

translations.definition: The practice of living as a parasite in or on another organism
translations.sentence: After they hatch from eggs, ticks survive through parasitsm where they eat blood from mammals.

definitions.definition: Bíla'ashdla'ii tádínéehgo bíla'ashdla'ii bił haz'ą́ągo yitah yinééh éí doodago nááná łahgi haz'ą́ągi niinééhgo

translations.definition: The movement of individuals into a particular population or area from other populations or areas. Compare with emigration.
translations.sentence: When you get a cut it is important to wash it with soap and water so you do not get an infection.

definitions.definition: Na'alkaahk'ehgo t'áá ałtsoní bízhi' dóó íiyisíí át'éhígíí baa hane'

translations.definition: The science of naming and describing organisms.
translations.sentence: A wolf pack has a large territory of up to 1000 square miles that it defends from other wolves.  If let outside, a house cat will defend a small territory of less than 10 square miles and will kill both pests and beneficial wildlife.
